In his project, 'Beach Epiphany,' he explores the beach as a microcosm of society, documenting moments that range from joyful and intimate to absurd and alienating. Koraicho's approach emphasizes observation and interaction, allowing spontaneous encounters with beachgoers to shape his photographic narrative. His work highlights the diversity of Miami, reflecting cultural identity, economic disparity, and the spectacle of everyday life. From children tumbling in waves to adults performing, flirting, or simply relaxing, Koraicho captures these interactions with a sharp eye for detail, revealing the beauty, desire, and contradictions that coexist in this coastal space. He describes the beach as a stage where vulnerability, boldness, and human impulses are laid bare. By combining patient observation with a sensitivity to composition and color, K

item.title: Controversy Over DOJ $1.776 Billion Fund to Settle Trump Allies' Claims
item.description: The Department of Justice has announced a $1.776 billion fund intended to settle claims from former President Donald Trump, his family, and associates who argue they were politically targeted by previous administrations. This fund, largely funded by taxpayers, comes as part of a settlement in which Trump, his sons, and the Trump Organization agreed to drop a $10 billion lawsuit against the IRS. Critics across social media and political circles have condemned the fund as unfair, citing concerns over its lack of transparency and oversight. Observers note that while some payments may go to individuals involved in the January 6 Capitol attack, much of the money is likely to benefit wealthy Trump allies who claim persecution. Legal and political experts argue that this could set a troubling precedent for using federal funds to settle politically motivated claims. Calls for congressional oversight and potential efforts to reclaim the funds have emerged, highlighting public concern about the

item.title: Behind the Scenes with Joan Collins on the Set of Seven Thieves (1960)
item.description: Released in 1960, 'Seven Thieves' is a classic heist noir film directed by Henry Hathaway, showcasing Edward G. Robinson as an aging criminal mastermind who organizes a team to pull off an ambitious robbery at Monte Carlo Casino. The movie is renowned for its careful pacing, tense atmosphere, and psychologically-driven storytelling, distinguishing it from modern action-heavy films. Joan Collins stars as a seductive dancer whose role is crucial in distracting others during the heist, while Rod Steiger plays the cynical son, adding depth to the ensemble cast. The film’s black-and-white cinematography enhances the tension, and the heist sequence itself is a hallmark of 1960s cinema, emphasizing meticulous planning, silent coordination, and the looming risk that human error could unravel even the perfect plan. Beyond its suspenseful plot, 'Seven Thieves' captures the contrast between the glamorous French Riviera and the desperation of men seeking one last score. These vintage portraits of

item.title: Trump Targets Thomas Massie in Kentucky Primary Over Spending, War, and Epstein Files
item.description: President Donald Trump has intensified his campaign against Republican Representative Thomas Massie, urging his followers to vote Massie out in the Kentucky primary. Massie, despite a history of conservative stances, has opposed several Trump-backed initiatives, including the 'Big Beautiful Bill' and U.S. involvement in the Israel-Iran conflict. The disagreement has extended to the release of government files related to Jeffrey Epstein, with Massie leading efforts alongside Rep. Ro Khanna to make the documents public. Trump has labeled Massie an 'obstructionist and a fool' on social media, while supporting Ed Gallrein, a former Navy SEAL, as a challenger. Gallrein has significantly less political experience and has declined Massie's requests for a debate. This primary will test whether Trump's endorsement and heavy financial support from aligned super PACs can unseat an incumbent in a solidly Republican district. Despite the high stakes and intense campaigning, polls suggest Massie has

item.title: Former GOP Strategist Highlights Republican Party's Ongoing Struggle with Trump Loyalty
item.description: Rick Wilson, co-founder of The Lincoln Project and former Republican strategist, highlights a fundamental flaw within the GOP: the party’s inability to reconcile principle with loyalty to Donald Trump. Wilson points to Senator Bill Cassidy's political experience as an illustrative case. Cassidy voted to impeach Trump during his first administration, then spent years attempting to regain favor by compromising his principles, including voting to confirm Trump-aligned cabinet members. According to Wilson, these efforts were futile because Trump’s loyalty demands are absolute and cannot be negotiated or appeased. This behavior, Wilson argues, represents the GOP's 'terminal blind spot'—a persistent misunderstanding among party members who believe they can balance integrity with allegiance to Trump. Wilson emphasizes that any perceived compromise or attempts at reconciliation are ineffective, as Trump views prior opposition as a permanent betrayal. The article sheds light on the ongoing tens

item.title: The Life and Work of Model Sandy Brown in 1950s Fashion
item.description: Sandy Brown was a highly influential fashion model during the 1950s and 1960s, celebrated for her elegance, poise, and classic features. She became a prominent face in leading fashion magazines such as Life, Vogue, and Harper's Bazaar, as well as appearing in numerous advertisements. Throughout her career, Brown collaborated with some of the era's most renowned photographers, including Lillian Bassman, Milton Greene, Richard Avedon, and Nina Leen, helping shape the visual identity of vintage couture. Her modeling repertoire included a wide range of styles, from intricate evening gowns to stylish daywear, showcasing her versatility and confidence. Iconic images capture her in works by designers such as Elsa Schiaparelli, Anne Fogarty, Carolyn Schnurer, and many others, often accompanied by luxurious accessories and meticulously styled hair and makeup. These photographs not only highlight Brown’s timeless beauty but also offer a window into the fashion and cultural aesthetics of the post

item.title: CAR-T Cell Therapy Shows Early Promise in Long-Term HIV Control
item.description: A small clinical trial has demonstrated that CAR-T cell therapy, a treatment previously used for certain cancers, could help control HIV long-term. In the study, researchers reprogrammed participants' immune cells to specifically target HIV. Two participants achieved undetectable levels of the virus, maintaining viral suppression for nearly two years and almost a year, respectively, without the need for ongoing antiretroviral therapy. The trial involved nine participants, some of whom did not respond as well, highlighting the importance of early treatment and proper conditioning for the CAR-T cells. This approach aims to create 'sentinel' immune cells that remain active in the body to prevent HIV replication. Unlike prior HIV functional cures, which relied on stem cell transplants from rare donors and were complex and risky, CAR-T therapy could eventually provide a safer and more scalable alternative. Challenges remain, including the high cost and labor-intensive preparation of CAR-T c

item.title: Exploring Algal Blooms in the Baltic Sea Through Art and Science
item.description: Małgorzata Stankiewicz’s book 'Viridescent, Afire' combines art, science, and mythology to examine the growing problem of algal blooms in the Baltic Sea. Using a blend of cyanotypes, satellite imagery, and poetic text, Stankiewicz visualizes the invisible threat of oxygen-deprived 'dead zones' caused by rising temperatures and human activity. The book transforms remote-sensed images from NASA and the European Space Agency into large, painterly prints, paired with free-flowing prose that spans science, psychology, and myth. Stankiewicz emphasizes the importance of creating a reflective space for readers rather than providing didactic information. Her work underscores the interconnectedness of humans and nature, illustrating the slow and largely unnoticed environmental changes that impact marine life. The artist’s method of translating complex scientific data into tactile visual art highlights both the beauty and destruction inherent in algal blooms, inviting contemplation and awareness.

item.title: ICE Tactical Trainer Linked to Multiple Fatal Police Shootings
item.description: David S. Norman, a former Phoenix police officer and founder of the law enforcement training firm TruKinetics LLC, has a documented history of involvement in multiple lethal police shootings. Norman, who described himself in a 2021 podcast as “a fucking savage” and openly sought high-risk encounters, was involved in six shootings during his time on the Phoenix Police Department, resulting in four deaths. After retiring in 2020, he founded TruKinetics, which provides training in advanced tactical skills to Homeland Security’s Special Response Teams (SRTs), including Customs and Border Protection and ICE units. The company offered a government-funded 40-hour course at Fort Benning, Georgia, used to train hundreds of SRT operators annually. Norman’s training focuses on tactical operations, close-quarters combat, and firearms proficiency, but he has publicly avoided discussing whether the training covers crowd control or active shooter scenarios. Concerns have arisen over the militarizatio

item.title: Why Chickenpox Parties Persist Despite Vaccines
item.description: Before the chickenpox vaccine, many parents intentionally exposed their children to the virus through 'chickenpox parties,' believing it was safer for children to contract the illness at a young age when complications were less likely. The varicella-zoster virus spreads easily via respiratory droplets and contact with fluid from blisters, making infections highly contagious. While most children recover quickly, some face severe complications, including pneumonia, bacterial skin infections, encephalitis, or meningitis. With the introduction of the vaccine, chickenpox cases have dropped dramatically worldwide, preventing thousands of hospitalizations. However, the idea of naturally acquired immunity has resurfaced in some online parenting groups, reflecting broader vaccine skepticism, especially in the post-COVID era. Experts warn that while vaccines have drastically reduced disease incidence, misinformation on digital platforms could lead to a resurgence of avoidable illnesses. Chickenp

item.title: A Retrospective on Brian Jones: Music, Life, and Legacy
item.description: Brian Jones (1942–1969) was a pioneering English musician and the founder of The Rolling Stones. Known for his multi-instrumental talent, he introduced unique instruments such as the sitar, marimba, and recorder to the band's sound, helping define their early musical style. Jones played a central role in naming the band and assembling its original lineup in 1962. Despite his early leadership, he was eventually overshadowed by Mick Jagger and Keith Richards, whose songwriting partnership became the driving force behind the group's success. Jones faced personal struggles with drug and alcohol abuse, which led to his dismissal from the band in June 1969. Tragically, less than a month later, at the age of 27, he was found dead at the bottom of his swimming pool, joining the infamous 27 Club of musicians who died at that age. In recognition of his contribution to rock music, Jones was posthumously inducted into the Rock and Roll Hall of Fame in 1989 as a member of The Rolling Stones. His li

item.title: Research Finds Rising Number of Fabricated References in Medical Publications
item.description: A recent study published in The Lancet has revealed a concerning rise in fabricated references in medical research papers. Researchers, led by Maxim Topaz, Ph.D., from Columbia University, developed an automated system to verify references in PubMed Central’s Open Access subset from January 2023 to February 2026. Out of over 2.4 million papers and 125 million structured references analyzed, 97.1 million references were verified, while 4,046 references across 2,810 papers were identified as fabricated. The prevalence of fabricated references increased sharply over time, from roughly one in 2,828 papers in 2023 to one in 458 papers in 2025, and one in 277 papers in the first seven weeks of 2026. This translates to a rise from approximately four per 10,000 papers in 2023 to 56.9 per 10,000 papers in early 2026. The study highlights a significant risk for medical professionals and guideline developers, as fabricated references can mislead clinical decision-making and are sometimes cited in

item.title: Trump Plans Permanent Helipad on White House South Lawn Citing Helicopter Needs
item.description: President Donald Trump is planning another major change to the White House grounds by installing a permanent helipad on the South Lawn, according to a Wall Street Journal report. This follows numerous renovations Trump has initiated since returning to office, including paving the Rose Garden, redesigning the Oval Office, and demolishing the East Wing for a new ballroom. The official reason given for the helipad is that the new VH-92A Patriot helicopter, which replaces the older VH-3D Sea King, produces exhaust that can damage grass and carries significantly more weight. Despite warnings from a 2018 Government Accountability Office report about potential lawn damage, Trump is opting to modify the landscape rather than adjust the helicopter. Plans for the helipad are reportedly being developed in consultation with Trump's associates. Critics argue that transforming the South Lawn, one of the most photographed areas of the White House, into a helipad treats a historic site like a private

item.title: How Two Traders Manipulated the 1955 Chicago Onion Market and Prompted a Federal Ban
item.description: In 1955, Vincent Kosuga, an onion farmer, and Sam Siegel, a businessman, orchestrated one of the most infamous market manipulations in U.S. history by cornering nearly the entire onion supply in Chicago. Using both physical purchases and futures contracts, the duo controlled almost 30 million pounds of onions, representing 98-99% of the city’s available stock. They pressured other growers to sell back inventory at inflated prices while secretly betting on a price collapse. To crash the market, they flooded it with their hoarded onions, some of which were temporarily removed and re-shipped to create the illusion of endless supply. By March 1956, onion prices fell from $2.75 per 50-pound bag to just 10 cents, leaving thousands of farmers bankrupt and millions of pounds of onions rotting, some dumped into the Chicago River. Kosuga and Siegel profited roughly $8.5 million, equivalent to about $100 million today. The scandal prompted congressional hearings, leading to the Onion Futures Act

item.title: Nvidia Shows Strong Revenue Growth and Attractive Valuation Despite High Market Cap
item.description: Nvidia (NASDAQ: NVDA) continues to attract investor attention due to its remarkable growth metrics and valuation. Trading at approximately 26 times forward earnings with a 65.47% revenue increase in the past fiscal year, the company presents a rare price-to-growth opportunity. Despite a market capitalization exceeding $5 trillion, Nvidia’s free cash flow reached $96.58 billion, and net income totaled $120.07 billion in FY2026. The company’s Data Center segment drove revenue growth, particularly its networking line, which surged 263%. Nvidia’s competitive edge stems from its dominance in CUDA software, NVLink, Spectrum-X, and other technologies, creating significant switching costs for customers. Major clients, including Meta and OpenAI, have committed to multi-gigawatt GPU deployments, ensuring long-term revenue visibility. Potential risks include a slowdown in AI capital expenditure, competition from Google’s TPUs, and geopolitical issues affecting supply. Nonetheless, Nvidia has cons

item.title: Trump Shares AI-Generated Image With Alien, Sparking Public Speculation
item.description: President Donald Trump posted an unusual image on Truth Social depicting himself walking beside a humanoid alien figure, which appears to have been created using generative artificial intelligence. The post did not include any explanation, prompting widespread speculation. Some pro-Trump commentators suggested that the image hints at upcoming disclosures related to extraterrestrial encounters, noting that the Trump administration recently released previously classified UFO files and is expected to release more soon. Reactions varied widely: journalist Ann Vandersteel described the post as high-level trolling, while author Michael Salla interpreted it as signaling potential government contact with extraterrestrials. Others, including Edward Dowd and Luke Rudkowski, dismissed the post as trivial compared to other issues like gas prices or the Epstein files. The post highlights Trump's ongoing use of social media to engage his audience and generate discussion around sensational topics, bl

item.title: The 1902 Baker Electric Runabout: A Pioneering Electric Vehicle
item.description: The 1902 Baker Electric Runabout is an early example of electric vehicle innovation, manufactured by the Baker Motor Vehicle Company in Cleveland, Ohio. Unlike the noisy and cumbersome gasoline cars of its era, the Baker Electric offered a quiet, smooth, and easy-to-operate driving experience, earning popularity among urban socialites and women. Its design included a lightweight frame, a set of lead-acid batteries, and a tiller for steering instead of a wheel. The car could reach speeds up to 14 mph (23 km/h) and did not require a manual gearbox or hand-cranking, showcasing advanced engineering for the early 20th century. Although electric cars eventually lost dominance due to limited battery range and the rise of gasoline engines, the Baker Electric remains a historical icon. Collectors prize it today as a beautifully crafted, whisper-quiet testament to the potential of electric mobility long before modern EVs became widespread. The article features a collection of photographs that hi

item.title: McArthur Wheeler's Infamous Bank Robbery and the Birth of the Dunning–Kruger Effect
item.description: In 1995, McArthur Wheeler, a 44-year-old man from Pittsburgh, carried out a bizarre bank robbery that would later become famous for its psychological implications. Believing that lemon juice could render him invisible, Wheeler smeared it on his face before robbing two banks. He did not wear a mask and even looked directly at security cameras. When the police arrested him later that night, Wheeler was shocked to find that he had been captured on camera, insisting, “But I wore the juice.” His belief was based on a misunderstanding of chemistry: lemon juice can act as invisible ink, only visible when heated, not as a way to evade cameras. Wheeler’s attempt to test this theory with a Polaroid selfie failed because he photographed the ceiling instead, which he interpreted as proof that the juice worked. The incident caught the attention of psychologists David Dunning and Justin Kruger at Cornell University. They studied how Wheeler’s extreme overconfidence, despite his incompetence, exempli

item.title: Amazon Launches AI-Powered Podcasts Through Alexa+ With Customizable Topics
item.description: Amazon has introduced a new feature for its Alexa+ AI voice assistant that generates podcasts entirely using artificial intelligence. The company announced that Alexa+ can now produce full audio episodes on a variety of topics, with two AI-generated co-hosts discussing the content. Amazon is emphasizing accuracy and reliability by partnering with major news organizations, including the Associated Press, Reuters, Washington Post, Time, Forbes, Politico, USA Today, and over 200 local newspapers. In an example provided, the AI hosts discussed music trends, highlighting the diversity of unsigned artists and various genres releasing music simultaneously. Alexa+ Podcasts are included at no extra cost for U.S. Prime members, while non-Prime users can access the service for $19.99 per month. Users can request a topic, preview the planned content, adjust the episode's length or focus, and receive notifications when the episode is ready via the Alexa app or Echo Show. Amazon also plans to expand

item.title: Bernadette Peters’ Early Breakthrough in Dames at Sea
item.description: Bernadette Peters first gained widespread attention for her performance as Ruby in the 1966 Off-Off-Broadway production of “Dames at Sea” at Caffe Cino. She reprised the role in the 1968 Off-Broadway revival, which transferred to the Theatre De Lys and ran for 575 performances. Peters’ portrayal of Ruby, a hopeful chorus girl from the Midwest who rises to stardom in a parody of 1930s movie musicals, earned her critical acclaim and a Drama Desk Award, establishing her as a major figure in musical theatre. She is also featured on the original cast recording from 1968/1969, performing songs such as “The Sailor of My Dreams” and “Star Tar.” Following her initial success, Peters continued performing in regional productions, including a 1973 run at the Paper Mill Playhouse. This pivotal role helped launch her Broadway career, leading to notable performances in shows like George M!, La Strada, and Mack and Mabel. The article provides a nostalgic look at rare photos from this formative period

item.title: Cambridge Researchers Develop LEDs from Electrically Insulating Nanoparticles
item.description: Scientists at the University of Cambridge have developed a new type of LED using materials previously thought impossible to power electrically. The breakthrough centers on lanthanide-doped nanoparticles, which are excellent at emitting highly pure near-infrared light but are electrical insulators, making them unsuitable for traditional LED technology. By attaching specially designed organic molecules called 'molecular antennas' to these nanoparticles, the team created a hybrid system that efficiently transfers electrical energy into the insulating material. These molecules absorb electrical charges, enter an excited triplet state, and then transfer energy to the lanthanide ions with over 98% efficiency, causing the nanoparticles to emit bright, highly pure light. The resulting LEDs, called 'LnLEDs,' operate at low voltage and produce a very narrow spectral output, making them ideal for medical imaging, optical communication, and highly sensitive detection applications. Early devices al

item.title: Lana Wood Discusses Her Role as Plenty O'Toole in James Bond's 'Diamonds Are Forever'
item.description: Lana Wood, the American actress and younger sister of Natalie Wood, is remembered for her brief but iconic role as Plenty O'Toole in the 1971 James Bond film 'Diamonds Are Forever.' The character of Plenty is portrayed as an opportunistic gold digger, first appearing alongside Sean Connery's Bond at a Las Vegas casino. Wood’s portrayal was influenced by actress Leigh Taylor-Young and even drew inspiration from the character Minnie Mouse, contributing to her flirtatious and memorable introduction, 'Hi, I’m Plenty,' met with Bond's classic line, 'But of course you are.' Originally considered for the main role of Tiffany Case, Wood was offered the part of Plenty on the suggestion of screenwriter Tom Mankiewicz. Filming presented challenges due to her height difference with Connery, requiring her to stand on a box for many scenes. In one dramatic scene where Plenty is found drowned, Wood's feet were loosely tied to a cement block, which nearly caused a real-life accident as the block drift

item.title: Minnesota GOP Senate candidate previously charged with domestic assault
item.description: Aaron Brutger, a Republican candidate for Minnesota State Senate, has a past legal history that has resurfaced amid his current campaign. In 2009, Brutger, then 26, was arrested and charged with one felony count of domestic assault by strangulation and one misdemeanor count of domestic assault after his ex-fiancée reported that he physically attacked her. The incident allegedly occurred during an argument over gas money, during which Brutger reportedly pushed her into her car, slammed the car door on her legs, dragged her back inside, and placed his arm around her throat, causing her to lose consciousness. Court documents indicate that he threatened her life and had previously pulled a gun on her. Brutger later pleaded guilty to a lesser misdemeanor charge of disorderly conduct as part of a plea deal. He received a suspended 90-day sentence, a $50 fine, and was required to attend counseling, conditions he completed. Now 42, Brutger is running for Minnesota's Senate District 13, a histo

item.title: Janis Paige: Celebrating a Legendary Career in Film and Broadway
item.description: Janis Paige (1922–2024) was a celebrated American actress whose career spanned over six decades, leaving a lasting impact on Hollywood and Broadway. Discovered during World War II while performing at the Hollywood Canteen, she quickly signed with Warner Bros., earning fame as the witty and vivacious leading lady in musicals and comedies. Her performances in films such as 'Silk Stockings' (1957) showcased her charisma and comedic timing, often alongside stars like Bob Hope. Beyond the screen, Paige achieved legendary status on Broadway, originating the role of Babe Williams in the 1954 hit 'The Pajama Game', a performance that solidified her reputation as a top-tier musical theater talent. Known for her remarkable stamina and professionalism, she continued acting well into her 80s, maintaining the charm and dedication characteristic of the Golden Age of Hollywood. Janis Paige's legacy lies not only in her memorable performances but also in her embodiment of an era where glamour, talent,

item.title: Insights and Techniques for Creating Compelling Portraits with Todd Hido
item.description: In this in-depth interview, acclaimed American photographer Todd Hido shares his insights on creating impactful photographic portraits. Hido emphasizes that the power of a portrait comes not only from technical skill but also from the emotions and personal meanings viewers bring to an image. He cites Richard Avedon's portrait of Marilyn Monroe as an example of an unguarded, deeply expressive photograph that resonates due to its authenticity. Hido advises aspiring portrait photographers to approach their subjects with kindness and thorough preparation, including scouting locations, anticipating lighting, and carefully considering wardrobe choices, as clothing can significantly influence the focus on a subject's face. He describes his own work as a blend of the subject’s personality and the artistic persona crafted collaboratively, often exploring memory, identity, and emotional nuance. Hido also notes that successful photography must convey an emotional hook and a sense of reality, whet

item.title: Wichita’s Garlic Salad: A Local Dish Deserving Broader Recognition
item.description: Wichita, Kansas, may not be widely recognized for its culinary contributions, but one local dish—the garlic salad—stands out as a unique regional specialty. Despite its bold flavors and refreshing qualities, the garlic salad remains largely unknown, even within Kansas itself. Originating from the now-closed Doc’s Steak House, which proudly advertised itself as the 'Home of the World Famous Garlic Salad,' this retro dish has been cherished by generations of locals. The salad is noted for its potent garlic taste combined with a surprisingly light and refreshing preparation, which has contributed to its loyal following. Food enthusiasts argue that such a distinctive dish deserves national recognition, not only for its flavor but also as a representation of Wichita’s local culinary traditions. While the restaurant has long since closed, the legacy of the garlic salad continues through the memories of those who experienced it firsthand, and it serves as a reminder of regional dishes that ma

item.title: Grafana Labs Confirms Code Theft but Refuses to Pay Ransom, No Customer Data Compromised
item.description: Grafana Labs, the company behind the widely used open-source web visualization software, reported a security breach in which hackers accessed its GitLab environment using a stolen token credential. While the token allowed the attackers to obtain repositories containing the company's source code, no customer records or financial data were accessed. Grafana has since invalidated the token and implemented stronger security measures to prevent similar incidents. The hackers attempted to extort the company by demanding a ransom to prevent the release of its codebase. Despite this, Grafana chose not to pay, citing FBI guidance against cooperating with cybercriminals, noting that paying does not guarantee the return of stolen data and may encourage future attacks. The incident differs from a recent breach at education technology company Instructure, which paid hackers after a series of network compromises. Grafana emphasized that its code is open-source and publicly available, making the impa

item.title: States Propose Holding Oil Companies Accountable for Rising Property Insurance Costs
item.description: As property insurance premiums soar due to increasing natural disasters, some U.S. states are exploring legislation to hold oil companies responsible for climate-related damages. In California, Hawaii, and New York, Democratic lawmakers have introduced bills that would allow state attorneys general or insurance companies to sue fossil fuel companies to recover costs associated with wildfires, floods, and hurricanes. While none of these measures have yet become law, they reflect growing concern over insurers withdrawing from high-risk areas and the rising reliance on state-backed insurance programs. The proposed bills follow broader trends in which states and municipalities sue fossil fuel companies for their role in climate change, using legal frameworks similar to those that successfully targeted tobacco companies for deceptive practices. These initiatives face strong opposition from oil companies and conservative groups, who argue that federal regulations protect them from state liab

item.title: Agriculture Secretary Faces Backlash for Promoting Beef Exports to China Amid U.S. Price Surge
item.description: U.S. Secretary of Agriculture Brooke Rollins faced significant criticism from MAGA-aligned figures after announcing new commitments from China to import American beef. Rollins celebrated the resumption of beef imports from 17 U.S. states, praising President Donald Trump for securing the deal. The announcement came shortly after reports revealed U.S. beef prices had reached record highs, topping $7 per pound for the first time, a 77% increase since 2020. Critics, including prominent conservative voices and former political candidates, questioned the logic of exporting beef abroad while domestic prices remain prohibitively high. Some commentators labeled Rollins the 'dumbest secretary' and called for her dismissal, arguing that the focus should be on making beef affordable for American consumers rather than feeding foreign markets. The spike in beef prices, sometimes referred to as 'beeflation,' is expected to impact a wide range of products, including fast-food offerings like burgers at

item.title: Katharine Hepburn’s 1937 Photo by Howard Hughes and Personal Letters Go on Sale
item.description: A rare photograph of Katharine Hepburn taken by famed aviator and filmmaker Howard Hughes around 1937 has recently resurfaced along with some of her personal love letters. These items, originating from Hughes' personal belongings, are being auctioned through Profiles in History. The letters reveal a passionate romance between Hepburn and Hughes, with Hepburn even referring to herself as his fiancée in one handwritten note. Despite Hughes’ repeated proposals, Hepburn never agreed to marry him, and their relationship ended before she met her lifelong partner, Spencer Tracy. In her autobiography 'Me: Stories of My Life,' Hepburn reflects on their three-year relationship, suggesting that ambition ultimately outweighed love in their choices. The auction of these personal items provides a fascinating glimpse into the private life of one of Hollywood’s most legendary actresses and her connection with a figure as intriguing as Howard Hughes.

item.title: Canon to close its last Philippine manufacturing plant due to declining laser printer sales
item.description: Canon Business Machines Philippines (CBMP) will cease operations at its laser printer manufacturing facility in Batangas by June 2026, marking the end of Canon’s manufacturing presence in the Philippines and affecting approximately 1,400 employees. The company cited global declines in office laser printer demand, evolving work habits, and the shift toward digital workflows as reasons for the shutdown. This closure is part of Canon’s broader strategy to scale back laser printer production globally, following earlier exits from China. Although laser printers remain a key product alongside office multifunction devices and cameras, Canon has seen declining sales in this segment, with the latest annual report indicating a 4.6% year-on-year drop, especially in Europe and China. The company plans to focus on inkjet printers, digital commercial printing, industrial solutions, and cloud-connected office services. The shutdown will only affect CBMP’s manufacturing operations and will not impact

item.title: Critics Raise Concerns Over DHS Posting Christian Message on Social Media
item.description: The U.S. Department of Homeland Security (DHS) faced widespread criticism after posting a Bible verse on its official social media account during a large evangelical-style event on the National Mall called 'Rededicate 250: A National Jubilee of Prayer, Praise & Thanksgiving,' which was endorsed by President Donald Trump. The post cited 2 Peter 3:18 and referred to Jesus Christ as 'our Savior,' prompting backlash from legal scholars, academics, and commentators who argued it violated the principle of separation of church and state. Critics pointed out that a federal agency should not appear to endorse a specific religious belief, as it serves citizens of diverse faiths and those with no religious affiliation. Experts, including professors from Georgetown and Johns Hopkins, emphasized that government messaging should remain neutral to uphold democratic values and protect all Americans. Social media reactions ranged from concerns about religious exclusivity to satirical commentary linking

item.title: Irish McCalla and Neal the Chimp Behind the Scenes of Sheena, Queen of the Jungle (1955)
item.description: On the set of the 1955 television series 'Sheena, Queen of the Jungle,' actress Irish McCalla formed a memorable partnership with Neal the Chimp, credited as 'Chim.' Neal appeared in 21 of the series' 26 episodes and was trained by Howard Bryant specifically for the show. Their interactions, often captured in candid photos at the World Jungle Compound in Thousand Oaks, California, became a key element of the series' charm. The photos frequently showed McCalla holding Neal's hand or peeking through jungle foliage, reflecting their natural rapport. McCalla performed nearly all of her own stunts in the first half of the series, including vine-swinging and wrestling mechanical alligators. Her demanding stunts eventually led to a serious accident, breaking her arm and injuring her knee. Due to her unique height and athletic build, the production struggled to find a female stunt double and ultimately employed male stuntmen in blonde wigs and leopard-skin costumes to substitute for her. Neal

item.title: Supreme Court Justices Align with Trump’s Solicitor General in Key Cases
item.description: U.S. Solicitor General D. John Sauer has significantly changed the role of the nation's top lawyer before the Supreme Court by adopting a highly partisan, Trump-aligned approach. Unlike previous solicitors general, who often balanced partisan considerations with broader federal government interests, Sauer has actively embraced a confrontational style that aligns with conservative justices and advances former President Trump’s executive power agenda. According to CNN correspondent Joan Biskupic, Sauer’s arguments have become unusually political, directly echoing Trump’s rhetoric in briefs and oral arguments. His influence is apparent in major cases like Louisiana v. Callais, where the Supreme Court significantly weakened the 1965 Voting Rights Act, and in pending cases such as Trump v. Slaughter, where he seeks to overturn longstanding precedents restricting presidential authority over independent agencies. Sauer’s elite credentials—including a Harvard Law degree and a clerkship under J

item.title: Democrats See Opportunity to Flip Georgia's 10th Congressional District in 2026
item.description: In Georgia’s 10th congressional district, Democrats are cautiously optimistic about flipping a seat that has been solidly Republican for over three decades. With incumbent Rep. Mike Collins running for Senate, the seat is open, giving Democrats Lexy Doherty and Pamela DeLancy a potential path to victory in the November 2026 elections. Recent electoral trends in Georgia and nationally, such as Democratic gains in state and congressional special elections, suggest a possible swing. While Trump’s low approval ratings and the district’s demographic changes favor Democrats, fundraising remains a challenge, with Republicans outpacing them significantly. Both candidates emphasize grassroots efforts, voter engagement, and policies that resonate across the district, from affordable living costs to healthcare access. They argue that flipping the seat is critical not only for local representation but also for potentially regaining control of the House and countering what they view as concerning n

item.title: International Response Underway as Ebola Outbreak Spreads in DRC and Uganda
item.description: Health authorities are mounting an international response to a new Ebola outbreak in the Democratic Republic of Congo (DRC) and Uganda, which has already caused hundreds of suspected infections and dozens of deaths. The World Health Organization (WHO) declared the outbreak a public health emergency of international concern, noting the high positivity rate and rising number of cases as a warning of a potentially larger epidemic. The outbreak involves the Bundibugyo strain, which currently has no approved vaccines or treatments. In the DRC, 10 cases have been confirmed, with 336 suspected and 88 deaths reported. Uganda has confirmed two cases, including one death in the capital Kampala. The United States is assisting with the relocation of a small number of affected citizens and has deployed CDC resources to support local containment efforts, including contact tracing, laboratory testing, and surveillance. WHO and other NGOs, such as Médecins Sans Frontières (MSF), are providing medical

item.title: How the Medicare Surtax Affects Middle- and Upper-Income Households
item.description: The article discusses the 0.9% Medicare surtax, a relatively obscure tax introduced under the Affordable Care Act in 2013, which could surprise many taxpayers. This surtax applies to high earners, with married couples paying it on income exceeding $250,000 and single filers over $200,000. Unlike Social Security taxes, the surtax is not clearly displayed on paychecks, leading to unexpected tax bills for dual-income households where neither spouse individually crosses the threshold. For example, a couple earning a combined $330,000 may only discover their surtax liability during tax filing, creating frustration. Politically, this tax is appealing because it allows lawmakers to raise revenue for Medicare without cutting benefits for seniors. The article warns that what starts as a 'tax on the rich' can gradually expand to affect upper-middle-class families, highlighting the potential for tax creep. Democrats may emphasize this tax during elections as a measure to fund Medicare, appealing

item.title: Report Highlights Behavioral and Health Changes in President Trump as He Approaches 80
item.description: A recent report has raised concerns about President Donald Trump's fitness for office as he nears his 80th birthday, citing observable behavioral and physical changes. According to The Atlantic's Jonathan Lemire, Trump has reduced domestic travel, favors extended periods of 'executive time' watching television, and often conducts public appearances seated rather than standing. Observers have noted prolonged eye closures during events, which the White House dismissed as normal blinking. Trump's social media activity has intensified, including late-night posting sprees on Truth Social, some containing misinformation or unusual content. His public remarks increasingly feature tangents and disinhibition, and physical signs like bruising on his hands have drawn attention, though attributed to medical reasons by aides. Despite these concerns, the White House maintains that Trump engages with the press more than any previous president. Public perception reflects skepticism: a Washington Post

item.title: Pamela Anderson Open to Romance Again Following Speculation Involving Tom Cruise
item.description: Pamela Anderson, the 58-year-old actress known for her iconic roles and recent career resurgence, is reportedly open to finding love once more. According to sources cited by the Daily Mail, Anderson has been focusing on rebuilding her professional life in entertainment and is now making space for personal relationships. This development comes amid rumors of a possible connection with actor Tom Cruise, 63. Reports suggest Cruise became interested in Anderson after viewing her critically acclaimed performance in the comeback film 'The Last Showgirl,' which has reframed public perception of the actress. Insiders claim the pair have been in contact and that a mutual spark has been noticed by those around them. Anderson was previously linked to her 'Naked Gun' co-star Liam Neeson. The two attracted attention during promotional activities for the film last year, and Anderson later confirmed they had a brief romantic involvement after filming concluded. On the other side, Tom Cruise's most re

item.title: Daniel Craig's Hairstyle Transformation for 'Our Friends in the North' (1996)
item.description: In 1996, Daniel Craig appeared with a distinctive long, blonde, and often unkempt hairstyle for his breakout role as George 'Geordie' Peacock in the acclaimed BBC drama serial 'Our Friends in the North.' The show spans multiple decades from 1964 to 1995, requiring actors to undergo significant changes in appearance to reflect aging and evolving fashion trends. Craig's character transitions from a hopeful young man in the 1960s to a troubled individual facing crime, homelessness, and addiction in London. The long, straggly hair was a deliberate stylistic choice to visually represent his character's descent and to evoke the hippy aesthetics of the late 1960s and 1970s. While Daniel Craig naturally had longer hair in his youth, much of the exaggerated length seen on the show was achieved using wigs. Fans have humorously nicknamed the series 'Our Wigs in the North' due to the noticeable use of hairpieces on the cast. The article also highlights the broader context of hair and costume desig

item.title: Iran Proposes Charges for Subsea Internet Cables Passing Through the Strait of Hormuz
item.description: Iran has announced plans to impose fees on subsea internet cables running through the strategically important Strait of Hormuz. According to state-linked media and Iranian military officials, companies like Google, Microsoft, Meta, and Amazon could be required to comply with Iranian law, while cable operators may need to pay licensing fees for passage, with maintenance rights reserved exclusively for Iranian firms. Although these companies have invested in some of the cables, it is unclear if the routes pass through Iranian territorial waters. US sanctions further complicate enforcement, making it likely that Iran’s announcements serve more as political signaling than immediate policy. Experts warn that any interference with these cables could have far-reaching consequences, disrupting global internet traffic, financial transactions, AI cloud services, and critical communications across Europe, Asia, and the Middle East. Analysts note that Iran’s approach reflects a broader strategy to

item.title: Journalist notes unusual redaction of Trump's image in released Epstein files
item.description: The Justice Department (DOJ) came under criticism earlier in 2026 for mishandling the release of files related to Jeffrey Epstein, particularly for improperly redacting or failing to redact sensitive victim information. Veteran journalist Alisa Valdes-Rodriguez highlighted one specific redaction that drew attention: a news photograph of former President Donald Trump had his face obscured with a black box, despite there being no women present in the image requiring protection. According to Valdes-Rodriguez, the DOJ's stated policy was to redact women to protect victims, while men would only be obscured if necessary to protect a woman in the same image. The unexplained redaction of Trump’s face has fueled speculation about whether it was a protective measure, a mistake, or an intentional act, raising questions about the thoroughness and impartiality of the DOJ’s file releases. While this single instance does not prove the existence of further incriminating material, the journalist sugges

item.title: LetinAR Develops Advanced Optical Modules for Wearable AI Glasses
item.description: South Korean startup LetinAR is pioneering the development of optical modules for AI-enabled smart glasses, a key component that determines usability, image clarity, and power efficiency. Founded in 2016 by longtime friends Jaehyeok Kim and Jeonghun Ha, LetinAR has spent the last decade perfecting its proprietary technology called PinTILT. Unlike traditional waveguide or birdbath lenses, PinTILT directs light precisely into the user's eyes, producing brighter images in thinner, lighter, and more energy-efficient lenses. This innovation addresses the major challenges in the wearable AI glasses industry, where every gram and battery hour counts. LetinAR has already secured $18.5 million in funding from Korea Development Bank and Lotte Ventures, with total funding reaching $41.7 million, and plans a 2027 IPO in South Korea. Its optical modules are currently used by companies like Japan's NTT QONOQ Devices and Dynabook, as well as in a Swiss AI-powered AR motorcycle helmet from Aegis Rider

item.title: AI Advances in Early Cancer Detection Using Multimodal Models
item.description: Recent reporting highlights the growing role of artificial intelligence (AI) in cancer detection and diagnosis. According to the Daily Caller News Foundation, AI tools like GPT-4 are showing potential in assisting physicians with earlier and more accurate cancer identification. In a 2024 randomized study with 50 physicians, GPT-4 alone outperformed both physicians using AI and physicians working without AI. Researchers at the University of Southern California reportedly developed an AI algorithm capable of identifying a few cancerous cells among millions of normal blood cells in roughly 10 minutes. Additionally, Mayo Clinic studies suggest AI can detect pancreatic cancer on routine CT scans up to three years before traditional clinical diagnosis. The American Cancer Society estimates that in 2026, approximately 67,530 people in the U.S. will be diagnosed with pancreatic cancer, with 52,740 projected deaths. While these AI advancements are promising for earlier detection and improved pr

item.title: Joe Rogan expresses concern over Theo Von's mental health; Von denies issues on social media
item.description: In a recent episode of The Joe Rogan Experience, comedian and podcaster Joe Rogan expressed serious concerns about the mental health of fellow comedian and podcaster Theo Von. Rogan highlighted Von's use of SSRIs and referenced past statements Von made about suicide, including an unaired Netflix taping where Von reportedly struggled and mentioned trying not to take his own life. Rogan described Von's recent behavior as involving emotional spirals about world events and stated he felt compelled to help his friend, noting he has known too many people who died by suicide unexpectedly. The two are prominent podcasting figures who have supported Donald Trump. Theo Von responded publicly on X (formerly Twitter), pushing back against Rogan's comments by using a blue cap emoji to imply Rogan was lying. Von wrote that the claims were 'mostly cap,' stated he is doing great despite life's challenges, and affirmed he is fine. The public exchange highlights tensions between two influential voices i

item.title: A Look at Honolulu’s 1975 Parades and Hawaiian Cultural Celebrations
item.description: The Honolulu parades of 1975 offer a vivid glimpse into Hawaii’s cultural and social life during the 1970s. Major celebrations like the King Kamehameha Day Floral Parade and the Aloha Festivals became significant events, attracting both locals and tourists to the streets of Waikiki. The parades were renowned for their colorful Pāʻū riders—women dressed in flowing equestrian capes representing each Hawaiian island—and elaborately decorated floats featuring tropical flowers such as plumeria, orchids, and hibiscus. Beyond the floral displays, these parades mirrored a broader Hawaiian Renaissance, a period marked by renewed interest in traditional Hawaiian music, hula dance, and language. The celebrations unfolded against the backdrop of Honolulu’s developing cityscape and vintage vehicles, creating a unique juxtaposition of tradition and modernity. Marching bands, hula halau, and the gentle sounds of steel guitars added to the festive atmosphere, embodying the Aloha spirit. Photographs ca

item.title: Independent Film Industry Faces Market Shift but Finds New Ways to Reach Audiences
item.description: The independent film industry is undergoing a significant transformation as traditional revenue models crumble. Once-reliable mechanisms, such as the pay-one television window, which enabled pre-sales and financing based on talent and concept, have largely disappeared. Streaming platforms now negotiate directly, leaving distributors hesitant to pre-buy films, especially high-budget projects. At the Cannes Film Market 2026, this has resulted in a slow pace of deal-making and a buyer's market with fewer committed buyers. In response, innovative distribution strategies are emerging, centered on building direct connections with audiences. Companies like Watermelon Pictures leverage community-driven campaigns using WhatsApp, local leaders, and social media influencers to promote niche films. Similarly, faith-based ventures like Angel Studios and shows like The Chosen demonstrate the power of audience engagement and grassroots marketing. Online creators, including Markiplier with Iron Lung a

item.title: Controversial Danish Vaccine Research Influences US Policy Amid Scientific Scrutiny
item.description: Peter Aaby and Christine Stabell Benn, Danish researchers with decades of work in Guinea-Bissau, have long promoted the idea of 'non-specific effects' of vaccines—suggesting that live vaccines like measles and tuberculosis improve overall child survival, while inactivated vaccines like DTP may increase mortality, especially among girls. Their claims have been widely criticized by the scientific community for methodological flaws, small sample sizes, and confirmation bias. Despite this, their research has gained prominence in the United States under Robert F. Kennedy Jr.'s tenure as Health and Human Services secretary. Kennedy has cited their work to justify cutting billions in US funding for Gavi, a global vaccine alliance, and altering US vaccine policy, including controversial plans to withhold hepatitis B birth doses in Guinea-Bissau. Danish authorities and scientists have raised ethical and scientific concerns, with investigations ongoing into data handling and trial methods. Criti

item.title: Graduation Speakers Face Mixed Reactions When Discussing AI
item.description: During the 2026 commencement season, some speakers discovered that talking about artificial intelligence (AI) at graduation ceremonies can provoke strong reactions from students. At the University of Central Florida, real estate executive Gloria Caulfield highlighted the 'profound change' brought by AI, calling it 'the next industrial revolution.' Instead of applause, her remarks were met with a mix of boos and cheers, reflecting a polarized response. Similarly, former Google CEO Eric Schmidt faced student criticism at the University of Arizona, with booing occurring even before his speech due to past allegations and continuing when he discussed AI's role in shaping the future. Not all mentions of AI drew negative responses, however; Nvidia CEO Jensen Huang spoke at Carnegie Mellon without incident, emphasizing AI's transformative impact on computing. Experts note that students’ reactions may be fueled by economic uncertainty, with a Gallup poll showing only 43% of young Americans beli

item.title: Techire SHS Students Receive Civic Education on Integrity and Rule of Law
item.description: On May 17, 2026, the National Commission for Civic Education (NCCE) conducted a civic sensitization program for students of Techire Senior High School in the Tano North Municipality of Ghana's Ahafo Region. The program focused on educating students about corruption, the rule of law, and ethical conduct. Mr. Joseph Oduro-Buabeng, Municipal Director of the NCCE, emphasized the importance of discipline, punctuality, and time management, highlighting these as essential values for personal success and national development. He encouraged students to respect authority, safeguard public property, and avoid unlawful behavior. Complementing this, Mr. Victor Osei from the Commission on Human Rights and Administrative Justice (CHRAJ) addressed topics including conflict of interest, accountability, influence peddling, and ethical leadership. The initiative, organized by the Municipal Directorate of the NCCE, received support from international partners, including the German Federal Ministry for Eco

item.title: Los Angeles Mayoral Candidates Focus on Latino Voters Ahead of June Primary
item.description: With Latinos comprising nearly 37% of Los Angeles' electorate, securing their votes is a central strategy for mayoral candidates ahead of the June 2 primary. Incumbent Mayor Karen Bass currently holds a significant lead among Latino voters, according to polls by UC Berkeley and UCLA, with 29% support compared to 16% for former reality TV star Spencer Pratt and 9% for City Councilmember Nithya Raman. Community organizer Rae Huang and tech entrepreneur Adam Miller trail with smaller shares. Bass’ advantage is attributed to her longstanding grassroots relationships and advocacy for immigrant families during ICE raids. Raman and other challengers are making efforts to connect with Latino communities through cultural events, Spanish-language outreach, and door-to-door campaigning. Analysts highlight that a large portion of Latino voters remain undecided, leaving opportunities for candidates to expand their support. Turnout in primary elections among Latino voters has historically been low,

item.title: New Poll Shows Declining Support for Trump Amid Economic Concerns
item.description: A recent CBS News/YouGov poll conducted from May 13–15, 2026, reveals that President Donald Trump faces significant political challenges as public frustration grows over his economic policies. The survey of 2,064 adults indicates that two-thirds of Americans believe Trump's policies are worsening the economy in the short term. Most respondents describe their feelings toward the administration's economic approach as either 'frustrated' or 'angry.' Even among Republicans, Trump's approval rating on handling inflation has dropped to 63%, reflecting concerns tied to rising oil prices amid the Iran conflict. Overall, Americans report that incomes are not keeping pace with inflation, the economy is perceived as uncertain, and many expect a potential recession or slowdown. While the electorate remains skeptical of both major parties’ ability to address the cost of living, Democrats hold a slight advantage over Republicans in perceived economic competence. The poll highlights declining public

item.title: Art Kane: Iconic Photographer Capturing Mid-20th Century Music, Fashion, and Culture
item.description: Art Kane (1925–1995) was a groundbreaking American photographer whose work left an indelible mark on music, fashion, and cultural photography. Originally trained as an art director, Kane shifted to photography in the 1950s, quickly gaining recognition for his bold, cinematic style and conceptual portraits. He is most famously known for the 1958 photograph 'A Great Day in Harlem,' which brought together 57 legendary jazz musicians on a Harlem stoop, creating one of the most iconic group portraits in music history. Kane's photographic approach combined dramatic lighting, meticulous composition, and emotional depth, capturing the personalities of jazz greats, rock icons, Hollywood stars, and high-fashion models alike. His images were regularly featured in top publications, including Esquire, Life, Vogue, and Harper’s Bazaar, showcasing both his artistic vision and cultural insight. Kane's fashion photography, often featuring models like Veruschka and Dolores Wettach, highlighted innovativ

item.title: Trump's Lawsuit Against the IRS Could Create a Taxpayer-Funded Settlement for Allies
item.description: President Donald Trump filed a $10 billion lawsuit against the IRS and the Treasury Department in January, claiming damages over the leak of his tax returns. Reports indicate he is prepared to settle the case by creating a $1.7 billion fund to benefit his allies, including individuals involved in the January 6 Capitol attack who were previously pardoned. This fund would draw from the Treasury’s Judgment Fund, which is intended to pay legitimate court judgments, raising concerns about transparency and oversight. The proposed settlement could also prevent IRS audits of Trump and his associates. Legal experts and the presiding judge, Kathleen Williams, have questioned the legitimacy of the president suing an agency under his own control. Historically, federal settlements for administrative issues have been far smaller, making this case unprecedented in scale. Critics argue that the settlement would serve as a government-funded mechanism to enrich Trump's allies while bypassing normal lega

item.title: The Life and Fashion Photography of Arnaud de Rosnay in the 1960s
item.description: Arnaud de Rosnay (1946–1984) was a French aristocrat, photographer, and extreme windsurfer whose life combined glamour and daring adventure. Initially making a name for himself as a fashion photographer for Vogue and Harper’s Bazaar, he captured some of the most iconic images of 1960s and 1970s style. His photographs featured models such as Marisa Berenson and showcased creative fashion designs in exotic locations around the world, from Australia to Mauritius and the Swiss Alps. Beyond photography, de Rosnay was an innovative windsurfer, credited with inventing the speed sail and completing challenging solo crossings of international straits, including the Bering Strait and the Strait of Gibraltar. Tragically, he disappeared at sea in 1984 during an ambitious attempt to windsurf across the Taiwan Strait, leaving behind a legacy that blends high fashion with extreme sports. The article highlights a selection of his stunning fashion portraits from the 1960s, demonstrating his eye for sty

item.title: Karen Carpenter on Stage: Highlights from the 1970s and Early 1980s
item.description: Karen Anne Carpenter, born March 2, 1950, was an iconic American musician best known as the lead vocalist and drummer of the Carpenters, alongside her older brother Richard. Renowned for her distinctive three-octave contralto voice, Karen was widely praised for her technical vocal skills and remains a celebrated figure in music history, earning a spot on Rolling Stone's 2010 list of the 100 greatest singers of all time. In the early 1970s, she considered herself primarily a drummer who sang, preferring the technical challenge and comfort of her Ludwig drum kit. Despite audience and management demands to place her in front, she often felt exposed without her drums. Her 1970s performances were characterized by flowing, modest gowns and an intimate connection with the audience, delivering her signature 'basement' alto tone with impeccable pitch and breath control even while moving. By the early 1980s, Karen's stage presence evolved into a more polished showbiz style, incorporating sequine

item.title: Intimate Street Portraits Capturing Vulnerability in Washington Square Park
item.description: Amy Horowitz’s photobook, 'A Walk in the Park? — Street Portraits from NYC,' is a deeply human collection of street portraits taken over five years in Washington Square Park and the West Village. Horowitz’s work focuses on revealing the vulnerability behind her subjects' bold self-expression. By instructing them not to smile, she avoids performative poses and instead captures raw, intimate, and honest moments. Many portraits feature direct, steady eye contact, creating a sense of trust and emotional connection between subject and viewer. The book highlights young adults, often showcasing a mix of confidence, defiance, anxiety, and low self-worth, reflecting the complexity of real-life experiences. Horowitz emphasizes the softer, more human sides of people who may initially present themselves as flamboyant or eccentric. Washington Square Park, a historic hub for artists and performers, provides a rich backdrop, blending the park's energetic, chaotic environment with quiet, introspective

item.title: Early Career of Margot Fonteyn: Portraits from the 1930s
item.description: Margot Fonteyn, born Margaret Evelyn Hookham on May 18, 1919, was a rising British ballerina during the 1930s who later became one of the most iconic figures in ballet and the prima ballerina assoluta of the Royal Ballet. She began her ballet training at the age of four and spent part of her childhood in China, where she studied under Russian émigré teacher Georgy Goncharov. Returning to London around age 14, she joined the Vic-Wells Ballet School, performing initially under variations of the name 'Margot Fontes' before adopting 'Fonteyn.' She made her debut in 1934 as a snowflake in The Nutcracker and had her solo debut a year later in The Haunted Ballroom. Frederick Ashton created a role specifically for her in Le Baiser de la Fée, highlighting her talent early on. Fonteyn quickly rose to prominence after Alicia Markova left the company, performing principal roles in classics such as Giselle, Swan Lake, and The Sleeping Beauty. During the late 1930s, she established herself as the co

item.title: Exploring the History and Impact of Photography in Children's Books
item.description: The exhibition “L is for Look,” currently on view at Lausanne’s Photo Elysée, explores the rich history of children’s photobooks and their role in education, storytelling, and visual literacy. Beginning with early examples from the 1930s, such as Mary Steichen Martin’s collaborations with her father Edward Steichen, the exhibition highlights how photography has been used as a tool for cognitive development and learning. The show also features Tana Hoban’s influential works, combining photography, illustration, and graphic design to teach children observation and engagement with their surroundings. Other notable contributors include Adam Broomberg & Oliver Chanarin, Annette Messager, Shigeichi Nagano, Tomi Ungerer, and Camilla ‘Ylla’ Koffler, showcasing themes ranging from animals and nature to social awareness and empathy. Interactive elements allow visitors to handle books and prints, emphasizing the tactile and immersive qualities of children’s photobooks. The exhibition also highlig

item.title: Human-Caused Wildfire on Santa Rosa Island Burns Over 10,000 Acres, Threatening Unique Species and Forcing Evacuations
item.description: A human-caused wildfire rapidly expanded on Santa Rosa Island, the second-largest of California's Channel Islands, burning more than 10,000 acres as of Sunday night, May 17, 2026. The fire, first spotted Friday night, destroyed a cabin and an equipment shed while prompting the helicopter evacuation of 11 National Park Service employees. Approximately 70 firefighters and park rangers are actively combating the blaze, which remains 0% contained. The incident poses a significant threat to six plant species found nowhere else in the world, as well as native wildlife including island foxes, spotted skunks, and elephant seals. Smoke from the fire has drifted into the Los Angeles area and is expected to linger through Tuesday, affecting air quality in the region. Santa Rosa Island, located about 26 miles off Santa Barbara, has been closed to visitors for the week as a precaution. The Channel Islands National Park Service is leading response efforts, with the cause still under investigation. T

item.title: Trump’s China Visit Highlights U.S. Economic and Corporate Pressures
item.description: Donald Trump's visit to Beijing in May 2026 has been portrayed as more than a standard diplomatic mission. The article suggests that Trump traveled not as the leader of a confident superpower, but as a representative of U.S. corporate interests facing economic challenges. Accompanying him were high-profile business leaders and billionaires, including Elon Musk, Tim Cook, Larry Fink, and executives from Blackstone and Goldman Sachs. The author argues that the U.S. trade war with China backfired, leaving American companies more dependent on Chinese markets than vice versa. Rising inflation, supply chain disruptions, and rare earth shortages have compounded pressures on U.S. industries, including Silicon Valley tech firms and the Pentagon. The piece portrays the summit as a reflection of declining U.S. imperial influence, highlighting the increasing role of oligarchs in shaping foreign policy and trade. It emphasizes the widening gap between the billionaire class and ordinary citizens, wh

item.title: Jennifer Jones’ Iconic Performance in 'Portrait of Jennie' (1948) Captured in Photos
item.description: The 1948 film 'Portrait of Jennie' showcased Jennifer Jones in a role widely celebrated for its ethereal and haunting quality. She played Jennie Appleton, a mysterious girl who appears to a struggling artist in Central Park. The role demanded that Jones portray Jennie at various stages of her life, from a young schoolgirl to a mature woman, all within a brief cinematic timeline. Despite being 29 during filming, she convincingly captured the innocence of youth and the elegance of maturity through subtle changes in voice, posture, and expression. Produced by David O. Selznick, who was deeply invested in her performance, the film employed cinematic techniques like heavy diffusion filters to give her a ghost-like aura. Her wardrobe, spanning from early 1910s children's clothing to timeless gowns, reinforced the sense that Jennie was a woman out of time. While the film's pacing was divisive upon release, critics praised Jones for grounding a fantastical story in real emotion, making the sup

item.title: French Authorities Report About 10 New Victims in Epstein Human Trafficking Probe
item.description: French authorities have reported that approximately ten new suspected victims have come forward in the ongoing investigation into the network of the late U.S. financier Jeffrey Epstein. The probe, focused on human trafficking, was launched after the U.S. Department of Justice released additional Epstein files in January 2026. French magistrates are investigating possible crimes committed in France or facilitated by French nationals. Paris prosecutor Laure Beccuau stated that around 20 victims had previously identified themselves following a February appeal for potential victims to speak up, but about ten of them are entirely new to the investigation. Some of these victims reside abroad, and authorities are coordinating meetings to accommodate their availability. Investigators are reviewing Epstein's computers, phone records, address books, and other documents from the case, with international cooperation requested as needed. French authorities had earlier searched Epstein's Paris apart

item.title: Analysis of Potential European Escalation in the Russia-Ukraine Conflict
item.description: The article discusses the escalating conflict between Russia and NATO, particularly focusing on the risk that the war in Ukraine could spread directly to European countries in 2026. It argues that Europe, especially Germany, is underestimating the seriousness of Russia's warnings. The author traces the origins of the conflict back to NATO expansion, unfulfilled promises to the USSR after the Cold War, and Western involvement in Ukraine's political changes. The piece highlights recent military developments, including drone warfare, strikes on strategic Russian assets, and preparation of European forces. Russian officials, including Dmitri Medvedev, Sergei Lavrov, and Sergei Karaganov, are cited as warning that Europe’s continued military support for Ukraine could provoke direct Russian action, potentially involving conventional attacks and, in extreme scenarios, nuclear threats. The article emphasizes that the tension is now higher than during the Cold War and criticizes Western media a

item.title: Arkansas Man Arrested for Threatening Walmart Shooting Over Hantavirus Concerns
item.description: Aaron Bynum, a 20-year-old man from Oakland, Arkansas, was arrested after allegedly threatening to carry out a mass shooting at a local Walmart if the country went into lockdown due to a hantavirus outbreak. Authorities said the FBI received a tip from another online gamer who reported Bynum’s threats, including an in-game recording. Using the information provided, investigators traced the account to Bynum and executed a search warrant at his home, seizing his computer and related equipment. Bynum was booked into the Marion County Detention Center on a $2,500 bond. The hantavirus outbreak in question originated from the MV Hondius cruise ship and has affected multiple passengers, with 11 cases reported by the World Health Organization as of May 13, including three deaths. Health officials emphasized that hantavirus spreads very rarely between humans, making widespread outbreaks unlikely. The CDC confirmed that the strain from the cruise, Andes virus, is the only type known to spread pe

item.title: Bulgaria Claims First Eurovision Victory Amid Boycotts Over Israel Participation
item.description: Bulgaria won the 70th Eurovision Song Contest held in Vienna, Austria, with Dara performing the song 'Bangaranga.' This marks Bulgaria's first-ever win in the contest, which featured 25 acts following a boycott by Spain, Ireland, the Netherlands, Iceland, and Slovenia due to Israel's participation. Dara's performance, known for its energetic beats and choreographed dance routine, captivated both the national juries and audience voters. Israel finished second, followed by Romania, Australia, and Italy, while Germany and the UK ranked near the bottom. The event was overshadowed by political protests, as hundreds marched in Vienna calling for a boycott and chanting in support of Palestine. Critics highlighted perceived hypocrisy in Eurovision’s inclusion of Israel while excluding Russia in 2022. Organizers noted the contest was held amid challenging times, and television viewership was expected to be lower than last year, with just 35 entries—the fewest since 2003. Despite the controversi

item.title: William P. Welsh’s Art Deco Posters from the 1930s Showcase Commercial and Artistic Mastery
item.description: William P. Welsh (1889–1984) was a distinguished American illustrator and painter whose career spanned multiple decades, leaving a lasting impact on mid-20th-century advertising and visual media. Known for his mastery of composition and color, Welsh seamlessly combined classical fine art techniques with the bold, eye-catching style demanded by commercial illustration. His diverse body of work included evocative travel posters for the Pullman Company and South African tourism campaigns, as well as sophisticated magazine covers for Vogue and Woman’s Home Companion. During the 1930s, Welsh’s Art Deco-inspired posters demonstrated streamlined forms and monumental elegance while maintaining a warm, humanistic touch. These posters promoted travel and leisure, highlighting winter and summer resorts, Pullman railway services, and seasonal experiences through bold graphics and refined aesthetics. Beyond commercial work, Welsh was also adept at creating large-scale murals and intimate portraits,

item.title: Linus Torvalds Discusses Challenges of Managing AI-Generated Linux Kernel Bug Reports
item.description: In a recent announcement on the Linux kernel mailing list, Linus Torvalds highlighted a growing issue with AI-generated bug reports. The flood of automated reports has overwhelmed the kernel security team, creating significant duplication as multiple researchers often identify the same bugs on the same day using similar AI tools. Torvalds emphasized that managing these reports on private security lists is largely pointless, as they are not confidential and only add extra churn. The kernel documentation now clarifies that many of these AI-reported issues are regular bugs incorrectly labeled as security vulnerabilities due to misunderstandings of the Linux threat model. Torvalds urged contributors to add value by reading the documentation, creating patches, and providing meaningful input rather than submitting random AI-generated reports without proper understanding. The discussion on Slashdot includes suggestions like using AI to filter and classify reports, but commenters caution that

item.title: Early Life and Career of Jean Harlow in the 1920s
item.description: Jean Harlow, born Harlean Carpenter on March 3, 1911, in Kansas City, Missouri, began her journey to stardom in the late 1920s as a teenage extra and bit player in silent films. Before becoming the iconic 'Blonde Bombshell' known for her platinum hair and striking screen presence, Harlow was a young socialite and occasional runaway from Chicago, exploring life in Los Angeles. At this stage, her hair was ash blonde or sandy, and her makeup and styling reflected the flapper trends of the era rather than her later, dramatic look. She appeared in several Laurel and Hardy shorts and other silent films like Moran of the Marines (1928) and Double Whoopee (1929), often in background roles and struggling with the demands of acting. Despite initial reluctance and attempts to quit, casting directors repeatedly recognized her natural charisma, eventually leading her to sign with Howard Hughes for the sound remake of Hell’s Angels in 1929. This marked the turning point in her career, transitioning

item.title: Poll Shows Declining Support for Trump and Growing Republican Challenges Ahead of Midterms
item.description: A recent CBS News/YouGov poll highlights significant challenges for President Donald Trump and the Republican Party as the 2026 midterm elections approach. According to the survey, two-thirds of Americans believe Trump's policies are worsening the economy, with widespread frustration and anger over his economic approach. Trump's overall approval rating has reached the lowest point of his second term, and even among Republicans, confidence in his handling of inflation has dropped to 63%, considerably lower than his approval on immigration issues. GOP pollsters and political commentators have expressed concern over these trends. Frank Luntz emphasized that 77% of Americans feel their income is not keeping up with inflation, while Sarah Longwell pointed to Trump's declining support among white non-college voters as a warning of potential voter disillusionment. Strategist Adam Carlson noted that while Americans generally dislike both parties, the current data shows significant dissatisfact

item.title: How to Encourage a Friend to Overcome Complacency and Take Action in Life
item.description: In this advice column, a reader expresses concern about his best friend and roommate, who is described as kind and gentle but also extremely timid. The friend has shared feelings of dissatisfaction with his career and personal life, particularly highlighting boredom with work and challenges in dating. Despite numerous conversations aimed at motivating him, he appears stuck in a state of complacency and fear of change. The reader seeks guidance on how to encourage his friend to take proactive steps to improve his life without straining their friendship. Carolyn Hax, known for her practical and empathetic advice, would likely suggest strategies that balance honesty with compassion, emphasizing the importance of fostering self-confidence and personal accountability while recognizing the friend’s temperament. The column highlights the broader challenge of helping loved ones break free from inertia and embrace meaningful change, and the emotional toll it can take on those trying to support

item.title: Kyle Rittenhouse Faces Backlash from MAGA Supporters After Endorsing Thomas Massie
item.description: Kyle Rittenhouse, the figure who gained national attention after being acquitted in the 2020 Kenosha shootings, is experiencing significant backlash from parts of the MAGA movement following his public endorsement of U.S. Representative Thomas Massie ahead of Kentucky's May 19 Republican primary. At a 'day at the range' event on May 16, Rittenhouse praised Massie for his conservative voting record and defended him against criticisms, highlighting Massie's involvement in the Epstein Files Transparency Act — legislation opposed by former President Donald Trump. The endorsement has put Rittenhouse at odds with Trump, who supported a challenger to Massie after the congressman opposed Trump's 'One Big Beautiful Bill.' Social media reactions from pro-Trump influencers were swift and harsh, criticizing Rittenhouse for supporting Massie. Some commentators pointed to past praise for Rittenhouse, highlighting the contrast with current criticism. Former Trump attorney Jenna Ellis defended Rittenh

item.title: President Nixon Flies Commercial Airline to Highlight Energy Conservation During 1973 Oil Crisis
item.description: On December 26, 1973, President Richard Nixon made history by becoming the only sitting U.S. President to travel on a regularly scheduled commercial flight. Nixon, along with his family, staff, and Secret Service detail, flew United Airlines Flight 55 from Washington Dulles International Airport to Los Angeles International Airport.
