The event was kept secret until shortly before departure, even the pilot was informed only thirty minutes prior. Notably, no other passengers were displaced to accommodate the presidential party. During the flight, Nixon walked through the aisles of the DC-10, interacting with fellow passengers, demonstrating a rare moment of accessibility for a sitting President. This unconventional choice was a symbolic gesture to address the 1973 energy crisis, showing solidarity with Americans and promoting confidence in the commercial airline industry. The presidential party purchased 13 first-class tickets at $217.64 each and 12 coach tickets at $167.64 each, totaling

item.title: Andie MacDowell Experiments with Sleek Hairstyles at Cannes Festival
item.description: During the 79th annual Cannes Film Festival on May 16, 2026, Andie MacDowell was seen debuting a fresh hairstyle, moving away from her well-known silver curls. She sported an elegant half-up, half-down look, with two wavy strands framing her face while the rest of her hair flowed in smooth waves over her shoulders. This appearance followed the previous evening’s event where she showcased a silky blowout paired with a black gown at the premiere of the film Karma. Earlier in the festival, MacDowell had worn her signature curls in a low ponytail with a pale blue dress. Since 2020, MacDowell has embraced her natural gray hair, citing her desire to follow her own aesthetic rather than conform to societal pressures to look younger. She explained that her decision was inspired by her father’s graceful aging and her wish to maintain her health and appearance without chasing youth. At Cannes, she paired her new hairdo with a sharp white suit and burgundy shirt, demonstrating both elegance and v

item.title: CDC Prepares Response as Ebola Outbreak in Congo Threatens International Health
item.description: A significant Ebola outbreak in the Democratic Republic of Congo and Uganda has prompted the World Health Organization to declare a public health emergency of international concern. In this outbreak, a small number of Americans may have been exposed to the virus. Officials indicate that at least one individual showing symptoms might require medical evacuation to receive proper care. The situation is considered unusual due to the size and severity of the outbreak, raising concerns among global health authorities. The CDC is reportedly mobilizing a response team to assist in containment efforts and provide necessary support to affected areas. Health experts emphasize that timely intervention and monitoring are critical to preventing further spread. The U.S. government is actively coordinating with international partners to ensure the safety of citizens and to support local containment measures. While Ebola outbreaks in Africa are not uncommon, the involvement of foreign nationals and the

item.title: Rhonda Fleming: Hollywood’s Golden Age Star and Philanthropist
item.description: Rhonda Fleming (1923–2020), famously known as the “Queen of Technicolor,” was a standout actress during Hollywood’s Golden Age. Renowned for her vivid red hair, striking green eyes, and flawless complexion, Fleming quickly became a favorite of cinematographers transitioning to color film. Discovered by legendary producer David O. Selznick, she gained widespread recognition with her role in Alfred Hitchcock’s 1945 psychological thriller Spellbound. Throughout the 1950s, Fleming demonstrated remarkable versatility, performing in film noir classics such as Out of the Past, lavish musicals, and rugged Westerns. Beyond her acting career, she was a talented singer, performing on Las Vegas stages and releasing her own album, Rhonda. Later in life, she dedicated herself to philanthropy, co-founding the Rhonda Fleming Mann Clinic for Women’s Comprehensive Care, focusing on advancing women’s health. Fleming’s life reflects a blend of cinematic glamour and humanitarian contribution, leaving behin

item.title: Two Teenagers Arrested After Multiple Random Shootings in Austin Injure Four
item.description: Over the weekend in Austin, Texas, a series of random shootings left four people injured and caused widespread fear across parts of the city. Two teenagers, ages 15 and 17, were taken into custody after police intercepted them driving a stolen vehicle, while a third suspect remains at large. Officials reported that the suspects targeted two fire stations, apartment buildings, and private homes while committing thefts and shootings. The firearm used had previously been stolen by one of the teens, and the older teen was already wanted for another firearm-related crime. In response to the threat, authorities initially issued a shelter-in-place order for a significant portion of southern Austin, which was later lifted after the arrests. Victims were hospitalized, including one in critical condition. Police and city officials emphasized that the attacks appear random, with no clear motive identified. The suspects also stole at least four vehicles during their spree. The incident has raised

item.title: Celebrating Contemporary Street Photography: 250 Inspiring Examples Worldwide
item.description: Street photography remains a vibrant and evolving form of visual art, capturing humanity in all its complexity. LensCulture highlights 250 contemporary street photographs selected by Jim Casper, editor-in-chief, offering a window into cultures, urban life, and the subtle beauty of everyday moments. From fleeting interactions to staged scenes, the featured works showcase creativity in composition, light, reflections, and the juxtaposition of architecture and human expression. The images span global locations and reflect diverse subjects, including locals, strangers, activists, and quirky characters. The LensCulture Street Photography Awards, launched in 2015, aim to recognize outstanding street photography worldwide, offering exposure, exhibitions, and cash prizes. Casper emphasizes that street photography not only documents reality but also connects viewers to the human experience, allowing for both engagement and introspection. This collection serves as inspiration for photographers a

item.title: An Overview of Lee Radziwill’s Fashion Evolution in the 1960s and 1970s
item.description: Lee Radziwill, born on March 3, 1933, and sister of Jacqueline Kennedy, was renowned for her distinctive and daring fashion sense, often considered more avant-garde than her sister's iconic style. In the 1960s, she embraced sleek, mod minimalism with a preference for designers like Givenchy and Christian Dior. Her wardrobe featured A-line silhouettes, shift dresses, structured coats, bold geometric prints, and low-heeled pointed shoes, complemented by simple accessories and polished hairstyles reminiscent of Audrey Hepburn. Moving into the 1970s, Lee’s style evolved into a more relaxed yet sophisticated aesthetic. She favored Halston and Valentino, adopting wide-leg trousers, wrap dresses, and luxurious fabrics such as silk and cashmere. Her signature look included tousled hair, oversized sunglasses, and an effortless jet-set glamour that made her a regular at Studio 54 and in elite social circles with figures like Truman Capote and Andy Warhol. Lee Radziwill was known for her minimali

item.title: Trump intends to install a permanent helipad at the White House to prevent lawn damage
item.description: President Donald Trump has announced plans to construct a permanent helipad at the White House, citing a recurring problem with the new Marine One helicopters. For years, U.S. presidents have boarded and waved from the South Lawn when departing via Marine One, the official call sign of the presidential helicopter. However, the latest generation of helicopters has caused unintended damage to the White House grounds, particularly by burning patches of grass during landings and takeoffs. Trump, a longtime helicopter owner, views the construction of a dedicated helipad as a practical solution to protect the South Lawn while maintaining the flexibility of presidential travel. The project underscores an ongoing effort to modernize the White House infrastructure and address operational challenges posed by updated aviation equipment. While the addition of a helipad is likely to improve safety and prevent damage, it may also attract scrutiny from those concerned about changes to the historic gr

item.title: Trump issues warning to Iran ahead of national prayer event
item.description: On May 17, 2026, President Donald Trump made a highly controversial statement directed at Iran just before attending a national prayer event in Washington, D.C. On his social media platform Truth Social, Trump warned that Iran must act quickly to reach an agreement or face severe consequences, implying potential destruction. This is not the first time Trump has made such threats toward Iran; previous comments included warnings of destroying civilian infrastructure and stating that 'an entire civilization will die tonight,' which drew widespread condemnation for being genocidal in nature. The national prayer event, called Rededicate 250, was organized by Freedom250, a group aligned with Trump and funded by millions in taxpayer dollars. Critics argue that these statements escalate tensions in the Middle East and could constitute violations of international law. Supporters frame the warnings as a firm stance on national security. The controversy comes amid ongoing political debates in the

item.title: Pedro Pascal surprises Disneyland visitors as the Mandalorian ahead of new film release
item.description: Pedro Pascal, known for his role as the Mandalorian in the Disney+ series, made a surprise appearance at Disneyland on Saturday, delighting fans aboard the Millennium Falcon: Smuggler’s Run ride. Dressed in full Mandalorian costume, Pascal revealed his identity to a small group of park visitors, joking about the reveal before taking photos with attendees. He was later joined by co-star Sigourney Weaver, director Jon Favreau, and LucasFilm President Dave Filoni at Galaxy’s Edge, Disneyland's 14-acre Star Wars-themed area modeled after the planet Batuu. The visit coincided with the press tour for the upcoming film 'The Mandalorian and Grogu,' a spinoff set to release on May 22, marking the first Star Wars theatrical release since 2019. The event highlighted the studio’s effort to generate excitement around the film while offering fans a memorable experience with their favorite characters in a theme park setting.

item.title: International University of Management to Launch 120-Bed Hospital and Training Facility Near Ondangwa by 2027
item.description: The International University of Management (IUM), led by founder and chairperson David Namwandi, announced plans to establish a 120-bed private hospital near Ondangwa, expected to open by mid-2027. The hospital, situated approximately 3 km northwest of Ondangwa, will not only provide medical services but also function as a training center in collaboration with Namibia's Ministry of Health and Social Services. This initiative is part of IUM’s broader strategy to expand its infrastructure and academic offerings across the country. Currently, the university operates campuses in the Ohangwena, Erongo, Khomas, Kavango West, and Oshana regions, serving a student population of 28,500 in the current academic year. IUM offers 122 accredited programs, including courses in nuclear science, gas, energy, nursing, and medical specialties. Beyond education, the university has invested in research and media, launching the Swakopmund Research, Training and Conference Centre, a water institute, and IUM

item.title: Analyst Highlights JD Vance’s Challenges in Gaining Influence Within MAGA Movement
item.description: JD Vance, currently serving as U.S. Vice President, has been considered a potential successor to former President Donald Trump within the MAGA political movement. However, recent analysis suggests his path may be more complicated than previously expected. Political commentator David Pakman highlighted that prediction markets show Vance's chances of winning the Republican presidential nomination in 2028 have dropped to 34%, down from over 50% earlier. By comparison, other figures such as Secretary of State Marco Rubio and pundit Tucker Carlson have odds of 30% and 6.3%, respectively. Pakman argues that Vance's perceived lack of charisma and entertainment value could make him a political liability for Trump, whose own political success heavily relies on public engagement and media presence. Despite being young, aggressive, and loyal to Trump, Vance's speeches and public appearances have not resonated strongly with audiences, potentially undermining his influence within the party. These f

item.title: Candid Photos Reveal Daily Life of an American Family in 1960
item.description: The early 1960s in the United States represented a transitional period between the structured domestic life of the 1950s and the transformative cultural changes of the later decade. This article showcases a series of candid photographs from Jane’s Album, offering a glimpse into the everyday life of a typical American family during this era. Suburban ideals dominated family life, with manicured lawns, station wagons, and the family gathering around the television set as central features. The photos highlight quiet, unscripted moments—children playing in the yard, family meals, and small household routines—that reflect the simplicity and routines of the period. These images not only preserve the nostalgia of the 1960s but also provide a historical perspective on how domestic life and family interactions shaped the American suburban experience. They illustrate the balance between postwar conformity and the cultural shifts that were beginning to emerge, providing a meaningful lens into mid

item.title: Cecil Beaton’s 1951 Vogue Portraits Capture Jackie and Lee Bouvier’s High-Society Debut
item.description:
In 1951, renowned photographer Cecil Beaton created a memorable series of portraits featuring the Bouvier sisters—Jacqueline Bouvier, later known as Jackie Kennedy Onassis, and her younger sister Lee, who would become Princess Lee Radziwill. Commissioned for Vogue magazine, the photos were carefully staged to evoke the elegance and ritual of a debutante ball, symbolizing the sisters’ formal introduction into elite social circles. At the time of the shoot, Jackie was about 22 years old and Lee approximately 18, both on the cusp of the public roles they would later embody.
Although Jackie had already been named “Debutante of the Year” in 1947, the Vogue feature presented the sisters together as a refined and fashionable duo. Their attire played a central role in reinforcing this image: Jackie wore a white silk organdie gown accented with a red faille sash, while Lee appeared in a yellow-and-white tulle dress adorned with yellow roses. While the designs were initially credited to Elizabe

item.title: Mike Johnson Comments on Gerrymandering and Voting Rights After Supreme Court Decision
item.description: House Speaker Mike Johnson (R-LA) addressed questions about Black voters following the Supreme Court's recent decision that limited key protections of the Voting Rights Act. During an interview with Fox News' Shannon Bream, Johnson stated that congressional district lines must be drawn fairly and cannot be based on race, referencing the court's ruling that struck down a gerrymander in Louisiana. Critics argue that this Republican-led redistricting effort has disproportionately harmed Black voters, with some calling it a form of political disenfranchisement. Johnson framed the court ruling and subsequent state map revisions as restoring fairness and certainty to the electoral process, a claim that drew sharp reactions from civil rights advocates. Commentators on social media described Johnson's phrasing as tone-deaf, noting that his use of the word 'certainty' suggested an intention to limit the influence of Black and brown voters in future elections. The controversy highlights the ongo

item.title: Trump criticizes former aide involved in efforts to secure Pentagon funding amid Iran war
item.description: President Donald Trump has publicly criticized Robert Karem, a former Defense Department official and current senior aide to Senate Republican leader Mitch McConnell, despite Karem advocating for additional funding for the Pentagon during the ongoing conflict in Iran. Analysts Eliot Cohen and Eric Edelman, speaking on the 'Shield of the Republic' podcast, described Trump's social media attack on Karem as misdirected, noting that Karem’s efforts aim to address shortfalls in defense spending partly caused by previous budget decisions during Trump's administration. Trump accused Karem of being a 'never Trumper' and suggested he influenced McConnell to reject changes to the filibuster, which would have helped advance Trump’s legislative agenda. The Pentagon’s split of defense spending into a base budget and reconciliation package has created uncertainty among lawmakers about the use of funds. Cohen described the situation as symbolic of broader disorder and chaos in defense funding decisio

item.title: Rev. Franklin Graham criticizes moral state of America at Trump event
item.description: During President Donald Trump's 'Rededicate 250' prayer event at the National Mall, Rev. Franklin Graham, son of the late evangelist Billy Graham, delivered a pre-recorded speech describing America as morally decayed and rife with sin. Graham cited societal issues such as transgender rights and the inclusion of men in women's locker rooms as examples of moral decline. He also expressed concern over pervasive violence in society, referencing crimes like rape and murder, and criticized violent video games. The speech drew attention amidst scrutiny of the Trump administration's handling of the war in Iran and threats of severe military action. Social media reactions highlighted criticism of Graham's focus on cultural and social issues while neglecting broader societal problems like corruption, war, poverty, and healthcare. Commentators argued that the speech emphasized bigotry rather than practical concerns affecting everyday Americans. The 'Rededicate 250' event was promoted as a histori

item.title: Catherine O’Hara: Career Highlights and Iconic Moments in the 1980s
item.description: Catherine Anne O’Hara (March 4, 1954 – January 30, 2026) was a celebrated Canadian and American actress, comedian, and screenwriter whose career spanned over five decades. She rose to fame on the sketch comedy series SCTV, where she became known for her memorable characters like Lola Heatherton and Dusty Towne, earning an Emmy for her writing in 1982. In 1980, she briefly joined Saturday Night Live but left without appearing in any episodes, returning to SCTV for the 1981–82 season. O’Hara made her feature film debut in 'Double Negative' (1980) alongside fellow SCTV stars John Candy, Eugene Levy, and Joe Flaherty. The 1980s marked her transition from a beloved Canadian television comedian to an internationally recognized film actress, with roles in 'After Hours' (1985) and 'Heartburn' (1986). Her performance as Delia Deetz in Tim Burton’s 'Beetlejuice' (1988) became iconic, and it was on that set that she met her future husband, production designer Bo Welch. The decade set the foundati

item.title: Brain Activity After Exercise Plays a Key Role in Building Endurance
item.description: New research published in the journal Neuron reveals that the benefits of exercise extend beyond muscles and into the brain. Scientists at the University of Pennsylvania discovered that certain nerve cells in the ventromedial hypothalamus (VMH), called SF1 neurons, remain highly active for at least an hour after exercise. This post-exercise brain activity appears to be crucial for improving stamina. In experiments with mice, blocking these neurons prevented the usual endurance gains, even though the animals exercised normally. Repeated treadmill training increased both the number and activity level of SF1 neurons, correlating with improved running distance and speed. The findings suggest that the brain’s continued activity after workouts helps the body adapt by optimizing energy use, potentially enhancing recovery and efficiency in muscles, heart, and lungs. Researchers believe this mechanism could lead to new strategies for helping older adults stay active, assisting patients recoveri

item.title: Library of Congress Adds 'Doom' Soundtrack to National Recording Registry
item.description: The Library of Congress has officially added the soundtrack for the iconic 1993 video game 'Doom' to its National Recording Registry, preserving it as an important cultural and musical artifact. Composed by Bobby Prince, the soundtrack combines heavy metal, techno, and ambient influences, creating a high-energy musical backdrop that has become inseparable from the first-person shooter experience. Remarkably, the music was composed before the game's levels were fully designed, showcasing Prince's ability to craft compelling tracks independently of gameplay. Prince, a musician and practicing lawyer, also leveraged MIDI technology of the time to balance music with in-game sound effects. The induction highlights 'Doom's' influence not only in gaming but also in digital music innovation. Alongside 'Doom,' other notable 2026 additions to the registry include Weezer's 'The Blue Album,' Taylor Swift's '1989,' Beyoncé's 'Single Ladies,' and the original 'Mambo No. 5.' The announcement recognize

item.title: Expert Warns of Potential Challenges to Midterm Elections Amid Trump's Statements
item.description: President Donald Trump has raised concerns about the integrity of the upcoming 2026 midterm elections, prompting warnings from experts and voting rights advocates. Trump recently stated that he would 'do anything necessary to make sure we have honest elections,' a comment interpreted as a potential threat to challenge election outcomes if Republicans face losses. Marc Elias, a prominent voting rights attorney, described the situation as a 'five-alarm fire,' warning that the statement signals a serious risk to democratic norms. Elias emphasized that Democrats may be relying too heavily on institutions like courts to protect the electoral process, which he says may not be sufficient given current political realities. Trump has also not ruled out deploying federal agents, including Immigration and Customs Enforcement (ICE) or the National Guard, to oversee polling places, a move that could further escalate tensions and undermine voter confidence. Experts argue that Democrats need to devel

item.title: Trust and Accountability at the Elon Musk vs. OpenAI Trial
item.description: The trial between Elon Musk and OpenAI has reached its closing arguments, centering on whether OpenAI acted improperly as it shifted toward a more profit-oriented model. A major theme highlighted during the trial is trust, particularly regarding OpenAI CEO Sam Altman. Musk's legal team questioned Altman about the truthfulness of statements he made during congressional testimony, especially regarding his equity in OpenAI through Y Combinator. Altman defended himself, citing passive investment through a venture capital fund, while acknowledging that his conflict-averse style may have caused misunderstandings. Tech journalists noted that trust is not only an issue for Altman but for the AI industry as a whole, given the opaque nature of privately held AI companies. The trial also contrasted Altman's approach with Musk's, pointing out different ways both handled inaccuracies in their public statements. Overall, the trial underscores the importance of transparency and credibility in the AI

item.title: Canadian Passenger on Hantavirus-Affected Cruise Ship Tests Positive, Officials Report
item.description: A Canadian who was on the MV Hondius, a cruise ship affected by a hantavirus outbreak in April, has tested presumptively positive for the virus. The individual, from Yukon, is one of four Canadians currently isolating on Vancouver Island after disembarking, and has only mild symptoms. The province’s senior health officer, Dr. Bonnie Henry, emphasized that the virus is very different from respiratory viruses like COVID-19 or influenza and does not have pandemic potential. Out of the six Canadians who were on the ship, two are isolating in Ontario, while two couples are on Vancouver Island. So far, none of the other passengers have tested positive. The cruise, which departed Argentina on April 1, recently docked in Tenerife, Canary Islands, allowing passengers to begin isolation. The ship is expected to arrive in Rotterdam, Netherlands, where the remaining crew will disembark. The World Health Organization recommends a 42-day isolation period for those exposed. Hantaviruses are typically

item.title: Nanoparticle Therapy Restores Brain Waste Clearance and Reverses Alzheimer’s Symptoms in Mice
item.description: Researchers from the Institute for Bioengineering of Catalonia and West China Hospital Sichuan University, along with collaborators in the UK, have developed a nanoparticle-based therapy that reversed Alzheimer’s-like symptoms in mice. Unlike traditional treatments that target neurons or plaques directly, these 'supramolecular' nanoparticles act on the blood-brain barrier (BBB), a crucial protective system that controls waste removal from the brain. In Alzheimer’s disease, the BBB deteriorates, leading to the buildup of toxic amyloid-β proteins and subsequent cognitive decline. The nanoparticles were engineered to mimic natural molecules that interact with LRP1, a protein responsible for transporting amyloid-β out of the brain. In experiments, just three doses led to a 50-60% reduction of amyloid-β within one hour, and long-term monitoring showed that treated elderly mice regained cognitive functions similar to healthy younger mice. The therapy also repaired blood vessel function in th

item.title: Behind the Scenes of 'Badlands' (1973) Featuring Martin Sheen and Sissy Spacek
item.description: The article provides an in-depth look at the 1973 American neo-noir crime drama 'Badlands,' which marked Terrence Malick's directorial debut. Starring Martin Sheen as Kit and Sissy Spacek as Holly, the film follows the story of two young lovers who go on a violent spree across the Midwest in the late 1950s, inspired by the real-life Charles Starkweather and Caril Ann Fugate murders. Holly narrates the story with a detached, almost dreamlike tone that contrasts sharply with the violent events unfolding on screen. Produced on a modest budget of around $300,000–$350,000, the film faced financial challenges, even pausing production temporarily so Malick could secure additional funding. Although set throughout the Midwest, the filming primarily took place in southeastern Colorado towns like La Junta and Trinidad. 'Badlands' is celebrated for its lyrical cinematography and its influence on the 'outlaw couple' genre. The film earned Martin Sheen a best actor award at the San Sebastián Interna

item.title: Dennis Hopper: Actor, Director, and Photographer of the 1960s
item.description: Dennis Hopper (1936–2010) was a defining figure of the 1960s, known not only for his acting but also for his work behind the camera as a director and photographer. After early career setbacks and a brief blacklisting by major studios due to his difficult reputation, Hopper studied Method acting and took roles in television and independent films. His career peaked with the groundbreaking film 'Easy Rider' (1969), which he co-wrote, directed, and starred in alongside Peter Fonda. The film became a symbol of countercultural rebellion and freedom, earning critical acclaim, a Cannes award, and an Oscar nomination, while also boosting Jack Nicholson's career. In addition to his cinematic work, Hopper was an avid photographer, capturing over 10,000 images of 1960s counterculture, the art scene, and social changes, using a Nikon camera gifted to him in 1961. His photography offers an intimate and artistic lens on the era, complementing his film legacy. Today, his photographs are celebrated in

item.title: USM Alger Wins CAF Confederation Cup After Penalty Shootout Against Zamalek SC
item.description: USM Alger claimed their second TotalEnergies CAF Confederation Cup title after an intense final against Zamalek SC that ended in an 8-7 penalty shootout victory. The two-legged final concluded with a 1-1 aggregate score, leaving the outcome to the nerve-wracking penalty kicks. Both teams demonstrated strong performance and resilience, but USM Alger maintained composure under pressure, ultimately securing the trophy. The victory in Cairo adds another prestigious continental title to the Algerian club's history and underscores its rising stature in African football. Zamalek SC, despite playing at home and pushing the match to penalties, fell just short, showing determination and competitiveness throughout the tie. This win highlights USM Alger's growing continental pedigree and marks an important achievement for the club, celebrated by players and fans alike.

item.title: Seven-Day Fasting Study Shows Molecular Changes Across the Human Body
item.description: A new study from Queen Mary University of London and the Norwegian School of Sports Sciences investigated how the human body responds to seven days of water-only fasting. Researchers monitored 12 healthy volunteers, analyzing roughly 3,000 proteins in their blood before, during, and after fasting. While the body quickly shifted from burning glucose to using fat within the first two to three days, major molecular changes did not occur until after day three. More than one-third of the proteins tracked showed significant changes, particularly those related to the extracellular matrix, which supports organs and neurons in the brain. Participants lost an average of 5.7 kilograms (12.5 pounds), mainly fat, with lean tissue largely recovered after resuming food. Genetic analysis suggested that the fasting-induced protein shifts could influence long-term health, potentially improving metabolic, neurological, and immune function. However, researchers also noted risks, such as increased inflamma

item.title: Marilyn Monroe’s Costume and Hair Preparations for Niagara (1953)
item.description: Niagara (1953) was a landmark film for Marilyn Monroe, showcasing her as a glamorous and seductive figure in a Technicolor noir thriller directed by Henry Hathaway. The studio, 20th Century Fox, invested heavily in shaping Monroe’s screen presence, particularly through wardrobe and hairstyle. Costume designer Dorothy Jeakins developed multiple dress options, including the iconic pink form-fitting dress, to emphasize Monroe’s figure and highlight her character, Rose Loomis, as both alluring and dangerous. Studio hairdressers experimented with different wave patterns, curl placements, and hair volumes to find the most striking look on camera. These tests reflect the meticulous planning studios undertook to cultivate Monroe’s image, which would play a crucial role in her later success, including in Gentlemen Prefer Blondes the same year. The film also features the famous long walk scene, which became an enduring cinematic moment. Behind-the-scenes photos of these costume and hair tests pr

item.title: Man Arrested in Tustin for Shooting Ex-Girlfriend and Fleeing to Mexico
item.description: Tustin police arrested 47-year-old Juan Marquez in connection with the fatal shooting of his ex-girlfriend, 42-year-old Sandra Fernandez. The incident occurred on Thursday near the intersection of Yorba Street and Medford Avenue, where Fernandez, an Anaheim resident and single mother of three, was found unresponsive with gunshot wounds and pronounced dead at the scene. Authorities reported that Marquez, a resident of Hawthorne, waited near Fernandez’s workplace and fled the country through the San Ysidro Port of Entry after the homicide. Following an arrest warrant, U.S. Customs and Border Protection detained Marquez as he attempted to return to the United States. He was then taken into custody by Tustin detectives and booked into Orange County Jail on a murder charge, held without bail. Fernandez had recently started a new job and was returning home from training at the time of the shooting. The case highlights issues of domestic violence and cross-border law enforcement coordination.

item.title: Eric Schmidt Faces Student Backlash During University of Arizona Commencement on AI
item.description: During the University of Arizona's 2026 commencement, former Google CEO Eric Schmidt encountered boos from the audience while discussing the impact of artificial intelligence. Schmidt opened by reflecting on how computer platforms have both empowered voices globally and, simultaneously, degraded public discourse by rewarding outrage and amplifying negative behavior. When he compared AI to the transformative effects of computers, parts of the audience responded with immediate disapproval. Schmidt acknowledged students' fears about the future, including concerns over AI, job security, climate change, and political instability. He emphasized that the future is not predetermined and encouraged the graduating class to actively shape AI development and societal outcomes. Despite repeated boos, Schmidt concluded by congratulating the students and urging them to take an active role in shaping the future. Video footage of the speech shows the crowd reacting strongly to statements about AI becom

item.title: Behind the Scenes: Robert De Niro’s Intense Performance in ‘The Deer Hunter’
item.description: In 1978, Robert De Niro delivered one of the most powerful performances of his career in Michael Cimino's film 'The Deer Hunter.' Portraying Michael Vronsky, a steelworker turned soldier, De Niro fully immersed himself in the role, demonstrating his renowned dedication and emotional depth. The production took place across Pennsylvania, the Ohio River Valley, and the jungles of Thailand, challenging the cast with both physical and emotional demands. Behind-the-scenes photographs from the set capture De Niro’s quiet focus and the weight of his character, offering a rare glimpse into the intensity of filmmaking at that time. These images not only document the creation of a cinematic classic but also mark a pivotal moment in film history when De Niro cemented his status as one of the finest actors of his generation. The photos highlight the grueling work, the dedication to realism, and the enduring impact of the movie, which remains celebrated for its dramatic storytelling and the actors’

item.title: Acting Attorney General Discusses Investigations Into 2020 Election Allegations Without Presenting Evidence
item.description: Acting U.S. Attorney General Todd Blanche stated in a recent Fox News interview that there is a substantial amount of evidence suggesting the 2020 presidential election was rigged against former President Donald Trump. Despite his claims, Blanche did not present any concrete proof to support the allegations. During the interview with Maria Bartiromo, he mentioned ongoing investigations in states such as Arizona and Fulton County, Georgia, emphasizing efforts to verify voter eligibility and the integrity of ballots. Blanche also referenced comments from White House Chief of Staff Susan Wiles, who previously claimed Trump won several states he officially lost. When questioned about the absence of public evidence and potential arrests, Blanche acknowledged the investigative process takes time and admitted that arrests are not guaranteed. He reassured the public that any findings would be communicated once investigations are complete but did not promise a definitive conclusion regarding wh

item.title: Iraqi National Arrested for Planning Attacks on Jewish Sites in the U.S.
item.description: Federal authorities in the United States have arrested Mohammad Baqer Saad Dawood Al-Saadi, a 32-year-old Iraqi national, for allegedly plotting terrorist attacks against Jewish institutions in Los Angeles, New York, and Scottsdale, Arizona. According to prosecutors, Al-Saadi served as a commander for Kata’ib Hizballah, a U.S.-designated foreign terrorist organization, and collaborated with Iran's Islamic Revolutionary Guard Corps. The arrest follows an undercover operation that intercepted Al-Saadi's plans, which included using explosives or arson to target Jewish centers. Court documents reveal that he allegedly directed 18 terrorist attacks across Europe within three months and intended to replicate similar attacks in the United States. Recorded conversations show Al-Saadi inquiring about operatives and payments to carry out attacks on U.S. soil. The U.S. Department of Justice has filed multiple terrorism-related charges, including conspiracy to provide material support to foreign t

item.title: Survival at Sea: The Story of Terry Jo Duperrault, the Sole Survivor of the Bluebelle Tragedy
item.description: In November 1961, 11-year-old Terry Jo Duperrault survived one of the most shocking maritime tragedies in American history. The Duperrault family from Green Bay, Wisconsin, including her parents Arthur and Jean, and siblings Brian (14) and Renee (7), embarked on a chartered sailing trip in the Bahamas aboard the yacht Bluebelle. The family hired Captain Julian Harvey and his wife Mary to guide their dream vacation. During the night of November 12–13, Harvey murdered his wife and the entire Duperrault family, possibly motivated by life insurance and an attempt to cover up the crime, and then deliberately sank the Bluebelle. Terry Jo woke to the horrifying scene and managed to escape on a small cork float. She drifted alone for 84 hours in shark-infested waters under the scorching sun, without food, water, or shelter. On November 17, a Greek freighter rescued her, her severe sunburn and dehydration highlighting the ordeal. Harvey, facing exposure, committed suicide the same day. Terry Jo

item.title: GOP Pollster Criticizes Lindsey Graham's Praise of Trump-Aligned Candidates
item.description: A recent interview with U.S. Senator Lindsey Graham (R-SC) has sparked sharp criticism from longtime GOP pollster and political commentator Sarah Longwell. During an appearance on NBC News' 'Meet the Press,' Graham suggested that Senator Bill Cassidy's (R-LA) primary defeat was beneficial for the Republican Party. Longwell described the interview as 'revolting' and expressed concern about what it reveals about Graham's character. Once a vocal critic of Donald Trump, Graham has shifted to supporting Trump’s agenda, including his controversial foreign policy in Iran and endorsement of candidates aligned with Trump's vision for the Republican Party. Longwell noted Graham's willingness to publicly flatter Trump, contrasting it with his earlier disdain, such as when he destroyed his phone in a video after Trump made it publicly available. Graham’s shift underscores the broader changes within the GOP as it increasingly reflects Trump’s influence. This episode highlights ongoing tensions with

item.title: Alex Smalley Holds Two-Stroke Lead Heading into Final Round at 2026 PGA Championship
item.description: The third round of the 2026 PGA Championship at Aronimink Golf Club saw intense competition, with the lead changing hands multiple times. By the end of Saturday, Alex Smalley emerged as the solo leader at 6-under par for the weekend, holding a two-stroke advantage heading into Sunday. A total of 13 players shared the lead at various points during the round, including notable names like Jon Rahm, Rory McIlroy, Ludvig Åberg, Xander Schauffele, Patrick Reed, and Maverick McNealy, highlighting the tournament's unpredictable nature. Aaron Rai surged into the solo lead temporarily, while Matti Schmid and Nick Taylor posted impressive rounds of 65. Scottie Scheffler, the world No. 1, struggled to climb the leaderboard but maintained hopes for the final round. The Aronimink course proved challenging, with several players commenting on its difficulty and strategic pin placements. With a record $20.5 million purse at stake, Sunday promises an exciting finish, with multiple players in contention

item.title: Behind-the-Scenes Glimpse of Jayne Mansfield and Joan Collins in The Wayward Bus (1957)
item.description: The 1957 film 'The Wayward Bus' brought together two iconic actresses of the 1950s: the blonde bombshell Jayne Mansfield and the elegant British star Joan Collins. Based on John Steinbeck’s novel, the film depicted a tense journey aboard a bus, contrasting Mansfield’s playful, vivacious persona with Collins’ refined and sophisticated presence. Behind-the-scenes photographs from the set offer rare insights into the actresses’ off-camera interactions, showing them relaxing in period costumes, sharing laughs, and preparing for their scenes. These candid moments highlight not only their individual charm but also the unique dynamic between the two stars, reflecting a blend of Hollywood glamour and everyday camaraderie. The film itself, a dramatic adaptation of Steinbeck’s work, was enhanced by the charisma of its leading ladies, making these vintage images valuable glimpses into the Golden Age of 20th-century cinema. For enthusiasts of classic films, these photographs serve as a fascinating

item.title: Vice President Vance Visits Maine to Promote Fraud Prevention and Support GOP Candidates
item.description: Vice President JD Vance visited Bangor, Maine, on May 14, 2026, to highlight the Trump administration's efforts to combat fraud and support Republican candidates in the state. During a speech at the Bangor airport, Vance criticized Maine Democratic Governor Janet Mills and former President Joe Biden, claiming Maine ranks third in the nation for fraud. He praised former Republican Governor Paul LePage as a strong advocate against fraud and signaled support for LePage's congressional campaign in Maine's 2nd District. Vance also offered conciliatory remarks toward Senator Susan Collins, acknowledging her independence and praising her role in representing Maine's voters despite occasional partisan disagreements. His visit reflects a strategic effort to balance appealing to the MAGA base while recognizing the political realities of Maine, a state with independent-leaning voters. The trip comes amid growing voter concerns over inflation, driven in part by Trump's war with Iran, and debates o

item.title: The Life and Art of Albert Chevallier Tayler: Capturing English Society and Sport
item.description: Albert Chevallier Tayler (1862–1925) was a prominent British painter whose work vividly documented English life from the late 19th to early 20th centuries. Initially influenced by the French naturalistic style, Tayler became associated with the Newlyn School in Cornwall, where he perfected en plein air techniques, portraying the daily lives of fishing communities with authenticity and emotional depth. His early works reflect a profound attention to light, shadow, and the quiet dignity of ordinary people. As Tayler's career progressed, he shifted toward a more refined and urban approach, gaining recognition as a portraitist and painter of sophisticated social scenes in Edwardian London. Beyond portraits and interiors, he became especially renowned for his dynamic and colorful depictions of cricket matches, which remain iconic representations of the sport. Tayler’s oeuvre spans intimate domestic settings, bustling markets, and sunlit sporting events, all rendered with impeccable techniqu

item.title: Fox News host Maria Bartiromo faces backlash for controversial weekend monologue
item.description: Fox News host Maria Bartiromo received widespread criticism after delivering a provocative monologue on her weekend program, 'Sunday Morning Futures.' During the segment, she claimed that her show has been among the 'leading critical journalists' in exposing government mismanagement, referencing the 'made-up Russia collusion story' and alleging a 'coordinated attack' by Democrats to conceal former President Joe Biden's mental health issues. Bartiromo's statements coincided with President Donald Trump's intensifying criticism of Democrats ahead of the 2026 midterm elections. Trump has attributed recent incidents of political violence to Democratic 'hate speech,' including an alleged shooting at the White House Correspondents' Dinner. Social media responses were swift, with commentators and political analysts mocking Bartiromo’s claims. Journalist Aaron Rupar compared her coverage to North Korean state media, while others accused her of spreading falsehoods in support of Trump. Some obse

item.title: Historic First Meeting Between Emperor Hirohito and General MacArthur in Postwar Japan
item.description: On September 27, 1945, Emperor Hirohito of Japan met with General Douglas MacArthur at the U.S. Embassy in Tokyo, marking a significant moment in postwar history. The 40-minute meeting laid the foundation for cooperation during the U.S. occupation of Japan. MacArthur resisted calls to try the emperor as a war criminal, instead working to maintain Hirohito's position to stabilize the nation. The famous photograph capturing this encounter shows a casually dressed, towering MacArthur beside a formally attired, diminutive Hirohito, symbolizing American authority while sparing the emperor public humiliation. This image shocked many Japanese at the time, as it challenged the long-held perception of Hirohito as a divine figure. The meeting and photograph became an instrument of social and political change, helping to transition Japan toward a democratic system and constitutional monarchy. Hirohito took the initiative to meet MacArthur, demonstrating a commitment to his people and the peaceful

item.title: Long Beach Pride Parade Goes On Despite Last-Minute Festival Cancellation
item.description: The 43rd Annual Long Beach Pride Parade took place on Ocean Boulevard on Sunday, drawing rainbow-clad crowds and political figures, including Mayor Rex Richardson and gubernatorial candidate Xavier Becerra. Despite the parade proceeding as scheduled, the accompanying weekend festival was abruptly canceled by city officials due to unresolved safety and permitting issues, according to authorities. Long Beach Pride organizers stated they cooperated in good faith to meet requirements. Attendees expressed disappointment and frustration, viewing the cancellation as particularly painful amid the current national climate regarding LGBTQ rights. Many saw it as a symbol of the challenges the community faces, though participants emphasized that pride itself cannot be canceled. The city organized a free alternative event at Bixby Park, ensuring celebration continued, while parade-goers carried signs affirming that pride comes from within and cannot be taken away. Long Beach Pride has a long histor

item.title: Axl Rose Arrested in 1992 Over Riverport Riot Incident
item.description: On July 12, 1992, Guns N’ Roses frontman Axl Rose was arrested at John F. Kennedy International Airport in New York after returning from a European tour. The arrest stemmed from an outstanding warrant connected to the 'Riverport Riot,' a chaotic incident during a 1991 concert in Maryland Heights, Missouri. The disturbance began when Axl tried to stop a fan from taking unauthorized photographs during the song 'Rocket Queen.' Frustrated with security, he jumped into the crowd to seize the camera, which led to him leaving the stage mid-performance. The sudden end to the concert sparked a riot, causing dozens of injuries and significant property damage. After evading authorities while touring Europe, Axl was met by U.S. Customs and police upon landing at JFK. He faced four counts of third-degree assault and one count of property damage. His legal team argued the arrest was unnecessary since he planned to surrender voluntarily later that week. Ultimately, he was extradited to Missouri and,

item.title: Successful Launch and Docking of Dragon CRS-34 to the ISS with EuroSuit Testing
item.description: On May 15, 2026, SpaceX successfully launched the Dragon v2 CRS-34 cargo spacecraft from the SLC-40 pad at Cape Canaveral Space Force Station, Florida, carrying nearly three tons of supplies to the International Space Station (ISS). After two previous weather-related delays, the Falcon 9 Block 5 rocket performed flawlessly, separating the Dragon at 22:33 UTC. The first stage, B1096, completed its sixth flight and landed safely on LZ-40. This mission marks the first time a Dragon cargo capsule has completed six missions, with capsule C206 previously flown on CRS-22, CRS-24, CRS-27, CRS-30, and CRS-32. After two days of orbital maneuvers, CRS-34 docked at the Harmony module's PMA-2/IDA-F port, where the seven-member Expedition 74 crew awaited. The cargo includes 2,948 kg, with 2,132 kg of pressurized cargo and 816 kg of unpressurized payloads. Notable experiments include CLARREO Pathfinder for precise Earth reflectance measurements and STP-H11 to study Earth's ring current. Among the pre

item.title: David Gilmour Worked as a Model Before Joining Pink Floyd
item.description: Before becoming the legendary guitarist and vocalist of Pink Floyd, David Gilmour briefly worked as a male model in the mid-1960s. In 1966, Gilmour lived in France and Spain while performing with his band Jokers Wild, a group that struggled financially and often played small venues or busked to make ends meet. Taking advantage of his striking looks, Gilmour secured modeling gigs in European magazines and catalogs. His appearance embodied the 'British Invasion' aesthetic, with high cheekbones, long hair, and a lean physique, which was highly sought after in fashion hubs like Paris and London. Gilmour later remarked that modeling often paid more than his music at the time. His modeling career ended when he returned to London in late 1967, after which he joined Pink Floyd. His entry into the band came as a temporary replacement for Syd Barrett, who was experiencing mental health challenges, but within a year, Gilmour became the band’s lead guitarist. This transition marked the start of hi

item.title: Apple plans privacy-focused Siri update with auto-deleting chat feature
item.description: Apple is preparing a major update for its voice assistant, Siri, which is expected to be unveiled at the Worldwide Developers Conference in June 2026. The relaunch aims to reestablish Siri's relevance in the rapidly evolving field of artificial intelligence. A central focus of the update is user privacy. Apple plans to introduce the first standalone Siri app, powered by Google's Gemini AI, offering a chatbot experience similar to ChatGPT. Unlike other AI chatbots, the new Siri will limit how long user data is stored and processed. One notable feature may allow users to automatically delete conversations after 30 days or one year, while still giving the option to retain them indefinitely. Despite emphasizing privacy, some critics point out that Google will handle parts of Siri’s security, raising questions about how privacy claims will be implemented. Apple's focus on privacy may also serve to offset certain limitations compared to competing AI assistants. Overall, the update represents

item.title: Val Kilmer’s Transformative Portrayal of Jim Morrison in The Doors
item.description: Val Kilmer’s performance as Jim Morrison in Oliver Stone’s 1991 film “The Doors” is regarded as one of the most dedicated and transformative in rock biopic history. Kilmer immersed himself completely in the role, studying hours of footage, learning Morrison’s baritone singing style, and mimicking his mannerisms so accurately that the director, Oliver Stone, reportedly struggled to distinguish between Kilmer’s voice and Morrison’s in recordings. To embody Morrison’s physical changes over time, Kilmer adjusted his appearance by gaining weight, bleaching his hair, and portraying the evolution from the early charismatic “Lizard King” to Morrison’s later years in Paris. Remarkably, Kilmer performed all the singing himself, impressing even surviving members of The Doors with his vocal authenticity. While reviews for the film were mixed, Kilmer’s performance received near-universal acclaim, with many noting that his on-screen charisma rivaled or even surpassed that of Morrison himself. The de

item.title: Australian Volunteers Resume Humanitarian Mission to Gaza Amid Risks
item.description: A group of 11 Australians has departed Türkiye as part of the final phase of the Global Sumud Flotilla mission, aimed at delivering humanitarian aid to Gaza and challenging Israel's naval blockade. This effort is part of a larger initiative involving around 500 participants from nearly 50 countries. The mission carries significant risk, as previous attempts have seen vessels intercepted by the Israeli navy, with crew members subjected to detention, violence, and theft of personal documents. Despite these incidents, activists remain committed to continuing their humanitarian work. Medical professional Dr. Bianca Pullman Webb emphasized the importance of the mission, citing ongoing violence and the need for safety and freedom for Palestinians, including her colleagues in the medical field. The flotilla's departure coincides with Nakba Day, linking the maritime mission with broader public demonstrations in Australia calling for humanitarian access and justice for Palestinians. The Global

item.title: David Crystal and His Influence on Mid-20th Century American Fashion
item.description: David Crystal was a pivotal figure in shaping American fashion during the mid-20th century. Although many associate his name with a single designer, he was actually the driving force behind the David Crystal Company, which grew from a small family business founded in 1905 into a major fashion empire. The company became synonymous with the 'American Look,' a style characterized by clean lines, high-quality fabrics, and an elegant yet approachable suburban aesthetic. By the 1950s, David Crystal had become a premier manufacturer, producing everything from refined day dresses to the famous 'Haymaker' line. One of the company's most notable contributions was bringing Lacoste to the U.S. market, transforming its iconic alligator logo into a symbol of status and the preppy lifestyle. Beyond sportswear, David Crystal garments were recognized for their tailored fit and lasting quality, even in mass-produced ready-to-wear lines. The brand's designs were featured in leading fashion publications,

item.title: Federal Reserve Chair Kevin Warsh Plans to Reduce Balance Sheet, Implications for Investors
item.description: Kevin Warsh has assumed the role of Federal Reserve chair, succeeding Jerome Powell, and he intends to significantly shrink the Fed's $6.7 trillion balance sheet. This is a major departure from the past 15 years, during which quantitative easing helped propel the S&P 500 to record highs. Warsh argues that the Fed's large balance sheet disproportionately benefits those with financial assets and wants to rely more on interest rates than direct market interventions. If he succeeds, bond prices could fall while yields rise, increasing borrowing costs for companies and potentially impacting stock valuations, especially for high-growth tech companies that benefited from low discount rates. Investors may consider shifting toward sectors like financials, favoring companies with strong cash flows and balance sheets, and moving to shorter-duration bonds to mitigate risk. Warsh's approach could also challenge the psychological 'Fed put,' where markets have expected Fed support during downturns. W

item.title: Remembering Tatiana Samoilova, Icon of Soviet Cinema
item.description: Tatiana Samoilova was a defining figure in Soviet cinema, celebrated for her emotionally rich performances and timeless beauty. Rising to international fame with her role in the 1957 classic 'The Cranes Are Flying,' Samoilova captivated audiences with her expressive eyes and haunting screen presence, earning the Prix Jury Spécial at the Cannes Film Festival. Her delicate features and charm often drew comparisons to Audrey Hepburn, but her intense, tragic depth was uniquely hers. Beyond her breakthrough role as Veronika, she delivered a memorable portrayal of Anna Karenina in the 1967 adaptation, further cementing her reputation as a legendary dramatic actress. Despite working primarily within the Soviet film industry, her influence reached global audiences, resonating from Hollywood to Paris. Vintage photographs of Samoilova at the peak of her career showcase a woman whose talent and beauty continue to inspire classic cinema enthusiasts worldwide. Her legacy remains a testament to the

item.title: Kyle Schwarber Hits 20 Home Runs Early in 2026, Showing Historic Power
item.description: Philadelphia Phillies slugger Kyle Schwarber is off to a historic start in the 2026 MLB season. Through his first 46 games, Schwarber has already hit 20 home runs, putting him on pace for an unprecedented 70-home run season if the trend continues. This would tie him with legendary sluggers Barry Bonds and Mark McGwire, who are the only players to have reached 70 homers in a single season. Unlike Bonds and McGwire, both of whom were linked to performance-enhancing drugs, Schwarber's rise could be the first of this magnitude without such connections. Schwarber's offensive surge is fueled by improvements in his launch angle and barrel rate, which are career highs this season. While some of his statistics, such as his home run to fly ball rate, suggest a bit of luck, his elite contact and consistent power hitting make him a serious contender for historic records. With this performance, Schwarber is generating optimism not only for his own season but also for Phillies fans eager to see a st

item.title: Generation X Band Photographed by Adrian Boot in West London, 1982
item.description: In 1982, renowned British photographer Adrian Boot captured the punk and new wave band Generation X during a professional photo session in West London. Boot, celebrated for his iconic images of the British punk and reggae scenes, including work with The Clash and The Damned, documented the band's evolution from their raw punk roots in 1976 to a more polished, rock star image in the early 1980s. The photos feature frontman Billy Idol and bassist Tony James against the gritty, industrial urban backdrop of West London, reflecting the band's stylistic shift toward mainstream new wave success. Generation X, originally formed in the mid-1970s, gained significant attention with hits that helped define the transition from punk to new wave. By 1982, the band's look had become more stylized, moving away from the aggressive punk aesthetic into a sleeker, commercially appealing presentation. Boot's photographs perfectly encapsulate this transitional period, blending the raw energy of London's stre

item.title: Hollywood Actresses in Shamrock-Themed Dresses: Vintage St. Patrick’s Day Publicity Photos from the 1940s
item.description: In the 1940s, Hollywood studios frequently produced holiday-themed promotional photographs of their actresses as a way to maintain public interest and generate festive publicity. For St. Patrick’s Day, this often involved shooting pin-up style portraits featuring stars wearing dresses adorned with shamrock patterns or posing alongside large shamrock props. These images, many in black and white, captured the glamour and playful spirit of the era while tying into Irish cultural celebrations. Actresses highlighted in these sessions included Maureen O’Hara, Maureen O’Sullivan, Angela Greene, Adele Mara, Alice Faye, Ann Blyth, Arlene Dahl, Ava Gardner, Carole Landis, Dorothy Malone, Janet Blair, Janet Waldo, Janice Carter, Judith Barrett, June Haver, Kathleen O’Malley, Martha Vickers, Mary Brodel, Mary Murphy, Olga San Juan, and Noel Neill. The photographs served a dual purpose: celebrating the holiday and keeping the stars visible in fan magazines and newspapers. This practice was part of

item.title: Sen. Chris Van Hollen Calls for Kash Patel's Removal as FBI Director, Citing Alleged Weaponization and Drinking Reports
item.description:
During an appearance on NBC’s “Meet the Press” on May 17, 2026, Democratic Senator Chris Van Hollen of Maryland stated that FBI Director Kash Patel should be fired for multiple reasons. Van Hollen expressed doubt that the FBI could function effectively under Patel’s leadership. He referenced reports alleging that Patel’s drinking has left him incapacitated at times, though he clarified he does not object to personal behavior unless it risks public safety or compromises the director’s duties.
The senator highlighted a recent clash during a Senate hearing where he confronted Patel over a report by The Atlantic detailing alleged erratic behavior linked to drinking. Patel has denied the allegations and filed a $250 million defamation lawsuit against the publication. Van Hollen further accused Patel of weaponizing the FBI by targeting agents involved in the January 6 investigations and the probe into former President Trump’s handling of classified documents at Mar-a-Lago. He noted that som

item.title: Journalists Criticize Trump Administration Over Potential Cuba Military Threat
item.description: A recent report by Axios, citing an anonymous U.S. official, suggested that Cuban leaders have discussed hypothetical plans to use military drones against U.S. interests in the event of hostilities. The statement has sparked criticism from journalists and commentators, who accuse the Trump administration of presenting potentially misleading information reminiscent of the lead-up to the Iraq War. Critics highlight that the supposed 'plans' were defensive in nature, intended only in response to a U.S. attack, and argue that framing them as an imminent threat could be a tactic to justify aggressive U.S. action. President Trump, already engaged in conflict with Iran, has reportedly considered extreme measures against Cuba, including sanctions that have severely affected hospitals and food supply, and even a potential abduction operation targeting Cuban leaders. The media response emphasizes the risks of repeating historical patterns of misinformation used to rationalize military interventi

item.title: Research Highlights Watermelon’s Nutritional and Heart Health Benefits
item.description: Recent research indicates that watermelon is more than just a hydrating summer fruit—it may play a significant role in improving overall diet quality and supporting cardiovascular health. Studies using data from the National Health and Nutrition Examination Survey (NHANES) revealed that both children and adults who consume watermelon have diets richer in essential nutrients such as dietary fiber, magnesium, potassium, vitamins C and A, and antioxidants like lycopene. These individuals also tend to consume less added sugar and saturated fat, aligning better with dietary recommendations. Additional clinical research has examined the effects of watermelon juice on blood vessel function. A small, randomized, double-blind study at Louisiana State University found that compounds in watermelon, including L-citrulline and L-arginine, help promote nitric oxide production, supporting healthy circulation and heart rate regulation, particularly during periods of elevated blood sugar. While the sam

item.title: Anthony Scaramucci describes Donald Trump's hidden psychological tendencies
item.description: Anthony Scaramucci, former White House communications director and now outspoken critic of Donald Trump, has provided insight into what he considers a key psychological trait of the former president. In a detailed post on X, Scaramucci described Trump as someone who can be charming and disarming in person, despite his public persona of attacking opponents. He pointed out that Trump often publicly criticizes individuals but privately seeks reconciliation shortly afterward. Scaramucci noted that Trump becomes uncomfortable when surrounded by sycophants and actually seeks honest pushback, citing Canadian Prime Minister Mark Carney as an example of someone Trump respects because he does not flatter him. According to Scaramucci, this craving for genuine challenge reveals a side of Trump that contrasts with his public image, suggesting that he struggles to admit this desire for constructive confrontation. The post underscores a nuanced view of Trump’s interpersonal behavior and the complexit

item.title: Blue Jays Pitcher Jose Berrios to Undergo Elbow Surgery, Season in Doubt
item.description: Toronto Blue Jays pitcher Jose Berrios is scheduled for elbow surgery to address a stress fracture in his right elbow. The procedure, set to be performed by Dr. Keith Meister, may be more complex if ligament damage is detected. The recovery timeline is currently uncertain, and Berrios’ 2026 season could be significantly impacted or entirely lost depending on the findings. Berrios has already faced a challenging nine months, including right elbow inflammation at the end of the 2025 season and additional issues during his rehab assignment this year. Toronto had initially placed him on the 15-day injured list to start the season. If ligament damage is found, Berrios could face either an internal brace procedure or a full Tommy John surgery, potentially affecting his availability into 2027. Contractually, Berrios has an opt-out clause but is unlikely to exercise it, meaning he will remain with the Blue Jays beyond this season. The team’s pitching rotation has already been strained, with in

item.title: Trump's credibility issues could influence 2026 midterm elections, attorney says
item.description: Attorney and columnist Alan Scott Bolden has highlighted potential political consequences stemming from President Donald Trump's credibility issues. Bolden argues that Trump's history of false or misleading statements, tracked extensively during his first term, has eroded public trust. Nearly 16 months into his second term, polls suggest that a significant portion of Americans question Trump's mental acuity and no longer take his statements seriously. Bolden claims this credibility gap not only complicates domestic policy planning and business decisions but also weakens the United States' diplomatic standing. Despite these concerns, Bolden suggests Democrats could leverage this vulnerability in the upcoming midterm elections, presenting themselves as a check on presidential misconduct. The article emphasizes the dual impact of Trump's behavior: a challenge to governance and an opportunity for political opposition. Bolden frames the issue as both a national security concern and a potent

item.title: Everyday Life and Culture in Northern Argentina During the 1930s
item.description: This article explores life in Northern Argentina during the 1930s, contrasting it with the European-style elegance of Buenos Aires. In provinces such as Salta, Jujuy, and Tucumán, daily life revolved around agricultural labor and a deep connection to longstanding colonial traditions. The article highlights the rustic beauty of the region through photographs by Wolfgang Wiggers, showing Andean villages with horse-drawn carts, bustling local markets, and vast tobacco and sugar cane plantations where laborers worked under the expansive sky. The images also capture intimate moments of families outside adobe homes, indigenous communities, and local figures engaged in daily tasks. The article provides a visual record of a region where indigenous heritage and Spanish colonial influence blended seamlessly, preserving a lifestyle largely untouched by modernization. Overall, the piece offers a vivid glimpse into the cultural and social fabric of Northern Argentina in the 1930s.

item.title: Rick Steves Reflects on His First Solo European Trip in 1973
item.description: In 1973, Rick Steves embarked on a transformative journey that would shape his future career and lifelong passion for travel. Freshly graduated from Edmonds High School in Washington, Steves left for Europe the day after graduation with little more than a ten-week Eurail pass, a large backpack filled with unnecessary items, and a budget of only $3 per day. This first solo trip, taken without parental supervision or a safety net, introduced him to the thrills and challenges of independent travel. Steves recalls returning home with minimal funds and a malnourished body, but a deep and enduring love for Europe. This journey became the foundation for his later endeavors, including his guidebooks, PBS travel series, and tour company, all of which emphasize immersive, budget-conscious travel. Over his adult life, Steves has spent approximately 120 days a year in Europe, living much of it out of a suitcase, demonstrating the long-term impact of this formative experience.

item.title: A Glimpse of 19th-Century Cornwall Through Photochrom Prints
item.description: In the late 19th century, Cornwall was a region of striking contrasts, balancing its industrial past with the burgeoning tourism brought by the arrival of the Great Western Railway. Once dominated by the tin mines of Levant and Botallack, the area began transitioning into a scenic destination known as the “Cornish Riviera.” Coastal villages like St. Ives, Penzance, and Port Isaac reflected a unique maritime culture, where life revolved around fishing, farming, and the rhythms of the sea. Artists from the Newlyn School were drawn to Cornwall during this period, captivated by the clear light and the authenticity of local life. Photochrom prints from the era document this transitional period, offering vivid visual records of landmarks such as St. Michael’s Mount, Tintagel’s King Arthur’s Castle, and various coastal harbors. These prints also highlight daily life in villages, the bustling harbors, and the rugged beauty of Cornwall’s cliffs and coves. Through these images, one can appreciat

item.title: WHO Declares Ebola Outbreak in Congo and Uganda a Global Health Emergency, Sparking Political Reactions
item.description: The World Health Organization has declared the Ebola outbreak in the Democratic Republic of the Congo and Uganda a Public Health Emergency of International Concern. The announcement triggered widespread reactions, including politically charged responses and conspiracy theories. Right-wing influencer Laura Loomer linked the outbreak to a recent incident involving NIH virologist Vincent Munster, who was reportedly stopped at a U.S. airport with undeclared pathogen samples from the DRC. Loomer demanded the dismissal of certain officials, restrictions on foreign nationals in U.S. labs, and the closure of the Rocky Mountain Laboratory in Montana. Actor James Woods suggested the outbreak might be exaggerated for political purposes, particularly to influence midterm elections and promote mail-in voting. Conversely, New York Times columnist Nicholas Kristof criticized the Trump administration for dismantling USAID and withdrawing from WHO, arguing that these actions leave affected regions dang

item.title: Weekly Curated Visual Culture Highlights: Art, Design, and Architecture Stories from May 11-17, 2026
item.description: This week's edition of Fog Signals from Moss & Fog presents a captivating collection of visual stories that explore creativity across multiple disciplines. The roundup features hypnotic kinetic typography by Syddarth Mate, created as part of the 36 Days of Type project, showcasing mesmerizing animated letters. In the art category, Ian Strange's provocative suburban interventions involve physically altering real homes through cutting, flooding, and burning to challenge notions of the domestic ideal. Underwater photography that mimics Baroque paintings stands out for its stunning visual illusion, blending reality with artistic mastery. Architecture enthusiasts will appreciate the Peridot Bar in Hong Kong, a glowing green jewel-like cocktail lounge located on the 38th floor of The Henderson, a striking Zaha Hadid-designed building. David Ambarzumjan’s paintings demonstrate how a single brushstroke can transform time, color, and subject matter, creating layered temporal experiences. On a l

item.title: Republican Sen. Thom Tillis criticizes Defense Secretary Pete Hegseth over plans to downgrade U.S. Army Europe-Africa command and sideline Gen. Chris Donahue
item.description: Sen. Thom Tillis (R-N.C.) publicly criticized Defense Secretary Pete Hegseth in a detailed post on X, describing recent Pentagon decisions as 'amateur hour at best and deadly at worst.' Tillis specifically condemned the reported plan to reduce U.S. force posture in Europe and efforts to force out experienced military leaders, including a move to downgrade U.S. Army Europe-Africa from a four-star to a three-star command. This would reportedly sideline Gen. Chris Donahue, a highly decorated combat veteran with over 20 deployments who was the last American soldier to leave Afghanistan in 2021. Tillis praised Donahue as 'one of our nation's finest warfighters' and urged Hegseth to prioritize meritocracy over 'mediocre yes-men' and 'political henchmen.' The senator's comments have drawn responses from former officials. Former Rep. Adam Kinzinger pointed out that Tillis had voted to confirm Hegseth, while others like former CIA officer Marc Polymeropoulos and ex-U.K. Defence Secretary Ben Wa

item.title: Cyd Charisse: Iconic MGM Dancer and Leading Lady of the 1950s
item.description: Cyd Charisse (1922–2008) was a defining figure of the 1950s Hollywood musical, particularly under the MGM studio. Renowned for her remarkable athleticism, elegance, and famously long legs, Charisse transitioned from a featured dancer to a leading lady, bringing technical ballet precision to Hollywood choreography. Her talent earned her roles alongside legends like Gene Kelly in 'Singin’ in the Rain' (1952) and 'Brigadoon' (1954), and Fred Astaire in 'The Band Wagon' (1953) and 'Silk Stockings' (1957). Both Astaire and Kelly considered her their favorite dance partner, praising her strength, style, and sophistication. Unlike contemporaries such as Debbie Reynolds or Marilyn Monroe, Charisse embodied a different ideal of femininity—tall, statuesque, and sophisticated. Costumed by designers Helen Rose and Adrian, she became a high-fashion icon, with MGM reportedly insuring her legs for $5 million in 1952. The 1950s solidified her reputation as one of Hollywood’s most graceful and technica

item.title: Automakers Shift Jobs to Focus on AI Expertise Amid Workforce Changes
item.description: The automotive industry is experiencing a significant shift driven by artificial intelligence. Companies like General Motors have laid off a portion of their IT workforce—over 600 salaried employees—as part of a strategy to recruit talent with AI-specific skills. This move reflects a growing trend where AI is reshaping employment: creating opportunities for some while eliminating traditional roles. The most sought-after skills include AI-native development, data engineering, cloud-based engineering, model and agent development, and prompt engineering. The overall impact on jobs is expected to be negative in the short term, as layoffs outpace new AI-focused hiring. Across the U.S., Ford, GM, and Stellantis have collectively cut more than 20,000 salaried positions in recent years, largely due to technological and AI-driven changes. Some companies, like Samsara, are successfully applying AI to create revenue-generating solutions, such as models that analyze truck camera data to detect pot

item.title: Three Killed and Several Injured in Oakland Pedestrian Crash
item.description: Three people were killed and several others injured late Saturday night when a speeding vehicle struck pedestrians at the intersection of 85th Avenue and International Boulevard in Oakland. The crash occurred around 11:15 p.m., according to a spokesperson for the Oakland Fire Department. Three victims died at the scene, while three others were hospitalized, including two in critical condition. Additionally, two pedestrians sustained minor injuries. Authorities reported that the driver collided with multiple cars before hitting pedestrians. The suspect attempted to flee but was stopped and detained by bystanders. The Oakland Police Department has indicated that further details about the incident will be released later. This tragic event underscores ongoing concerns about traffic safety and pedestrian vulnerability in busy urban areas. The local community has expressed shock, and officials are likely to review measures to prevent similar incidents in the future.

item.title: Lynn Redgrave: A Portrait of Her Early Career and Style in the 1960s
item.description: Lynn Redgrave, born on March 8, 1943, emerged in the 1960s as a unique and unconventional presence in British cinema and theatre. While her sister Vanessa embodied classical beauty, Lynn became the face of quirky, relatable femininity in Swinging London. Her early career flourished on stage, where she was a founding member of Britain’s National Theatre under Laurence Olivier and excelled in productions such as Hamlet and Much Ado About Nothing. Redgrave’s comic talent was evident in her performance as a scatterbrained flapper in Noël Coward’s Hay Fever, where she held her own alongside established actresses like Maggie Smith. In 1966, she gained international fame for her Academy Award-nominated role in Georgy Girl, portraying an independent, free-thinking woman who defied the era’s glamorized beauty standards. She later starred in Smashing Time (1967), a colorful satire capturing the essence of London’s mod culture. Redgrave’s style in the 1960s featured expressive eyes, voluminous ha

item.title: Chelsea Fans Express Frustration as Team Struggles Under Boehly Ownership
item.description: Since Todd Boehly and the BlueCo consortium took over Chelsea in May 2022, expectations were high that the club would invest heavily and maintain its status at the top of English football. However, four years later, fans are increasingly frustrated by what they see as a lack of clear direction and consistent results. Chelsea currently sits in ninth place in the league, having missed out on Champions League qualification. Despite a surprising run to the FA Cup final, the team lost 1-0 to Manchester City, leaving supporters disappointed. Fans staged a protest outside Wembley, criticizing the ownership and calling for change. Boehly’s tenure has seen over $1 billion spent on player transfers, yet the squad remains bloated, lacking experience, leadership, and cohesion. Interim manager Calum McFarlane led the team for the final, but the club has cycled through several managers in recent months, including Liam Rosenior and Enzo Maresca, highlighting instability at the top. Chelsea has announ

item.title: James Carville calls Trump’s admission about the Iran war a historic presidential misstep
item.description: Democratic strategist James Carville described President Donald Trump’s recent remarks regarding the U.S. war in Iran as potentially the 'greatest' presidential gaffe in history. During a White House press event, Trump admitted that he did not consider the financial impact on Americans when making decisions about the unpopular war. Carville explained that such a statement represents an 'admission against interest,' which political advisors typically warn against, because it confirms a negative perception among the public.
